name: Tidequay Market
description: The broad stone plaza of Tidequay Market bustles with the commerce of Branmill Cove, its cobblestones worn smooth by generations of boots and barrow-wheels. Stalls of grain, dried fish, and Riverstone Amulets crowd the space, and the scent of salt and fresh-baked bread mingles in the air. Merchants call out prices in three languages while Pekakarlik Dwarves haul chain-rigged crates from the dockside below. The Mithril Chain anchor-posts rise at the plaza's southern edge, rune-inscribed iron gleaming even on overcast days.

name: Concordant Hall - Entrance
description: Tall stone doors carved with waves and sheaves of grain mark the entrance to Concordant Hall, the seat of Branmill Cove's three-Reeve council. The lintel bears the motto of the Eke Concordant in worn High Kin script: 'One Chain, Many Hands.' Two civic guards stand at their posts with the practiced stillness of those who have heard every argument twice. The hall's facade is imposing but not unwelcoming, its stone pale and dressed with the precision that marks Pekakarlik work.

name: Concordant Hall - Council Chamber
description: The main chamber of Concordant Hall rises two stories, its walls covered in murals depicting the Eke Concordant era: Halflings and Dwarves working the tidal fields together, barges riding high-water storms along the Mithril Chains, and the great Tide Turn harvest. On the east wall, a new mosaic added since the Deceivers' War shows Cinvarin's five witnesses, their sacrifice rendered in gold and crimson tile. Three oak chairs face a gallery where citizens may speak, and every word spoken here echoes with the weight of what disunity cost this region.

name: Runedock - Chain Anchorage
description: The Runedock smells of iron, brine, and the distinctive mineral tang of active rune-work. Massive Mithril Chain links as thick as a dwarf's forearm descend from iron stanchions into the Kailian's shallows, each link inscribed with elemental runes that have held fast since the Age of Giants. Pekakarlik engineers move along the dock in small groups, checking tension gauges and rubbing maintenance oil into the rune-channels. A shrine to Gonmareck Ritler stands between two forge-stations, a hammer-and-chain motif carved into its base.

name: Seawind Spire - Base
description: The square base of Seawind Spire is built from the same pale stone as Concordant Hall, its corners reinforced with iron brackets stamped with wave-patterns. A duty roster is chalked on a board beside the door, and a rack of signal horns hangs inside the entrance passage. The spire overlooks the lake approaches and the chain-fields to the north, and the watchmen stationed here take their work seriously â€” a dead-Kin-sense report from any direction triggers an immediate alert to the Reeve Council.

name: Concordant Hall - Records Annex
description: Shelves of bound ledgers and rolled wind-silk scrolls line every wall of the Records Annex, organized by year and season according to the tidal calendar. The annex smells of lamp oil and old vellum, and a single archivist works at a slanted desk by the window, cross-referencing chain-maintenance logs with harvest yield figures. Old maps of the Kailian chain-field positions are pinned to a cork board, some showing anchor points that have since been lost or shifted. The oldest records, sealed behind glass, date to the first generation after the Eke Concordant.

name: Runedock - Eastern Quay
description: Planting barges sit in their low-water berths along the eastern quay, their flat hulls designed to ride the Mithril Chains down to the exposed seabed when the Kailian recedes. The chain-guides â€” iron rings bolted to each barge's hull â€” are inspected daily, and any that show wear are replaced before the next Tide Turn. A group of Eruskan field-workers is loading seed-sacks into a barge's hold, calling measurements to a Pekakarlik engineer who tallies on a wax tablet.

name: Runedock - Western Quay
description: The western quay handles the heavier freight: grain sacks, fish barrels, and the large crates of Riverstone Amulets that represent Branmill Cove's most valuable export. A crane operated by a chain-and-counterweight system lifts loads from barges to the dock-level warehouse behind. The crane's mechanism bears Pekakarlik maker-marks three generations old, maintained and repaired but never replaced. The rune-anchor for Chain Seven rises from the water here, its inscriptions worn but still faintly glowing with earth-energy.

name: Seawind Spire - Watchtower
description: From the top of Seawind Spire, the full expanse of the northern Kailian stretches out under open sky. On clear days the far shore is just visible as a dark line, and the tidal flats to the northwest shine silver when the water is low. The watchman on duty sweeps the horizon with a brass glass, noting the position of chain-barges and any vessel moving without the standard flag-pattern. A wind-gauge and rain-gauge are mounted on the parapet, their readings logged every two hours. The view is commanding, and the wind up here is constant.

name: Tidequay Fishers' Row
description: A narrow lane behind the eastern quay where the fishing families of Branmill Cove keep their tackle, their mending frames, and their drying racks. Nets hung between posts create a canopy that filters the light into shifting green patterns. The smell of fish and rope-tar is overwhelming here, but the fishers themselves seem not to notice, working with the swift economy of people who have done this work since childhood. A small hearth-niche in the lane's end wall holds a clay figure of Semyon the Hearth Mother, wreathed in dried flowers.

name: Runedock Warehouse
description: The warehouse behind the western quay is a long, low building with thick stone walls that keep the interior cool even in summer. Aisles of stacked crates and barrels run its length, organized by cargo type. A night-watch roster is posted at the door, and the padlocks on the high-value storage room are Pekakarlik work â€” triple-tumbler mechanisms that a common thief would struggle with. Dockhands work through the day in organized shifts, their movements coordinated by a Halfling foreman who tracks inventory on a hanging abacus.

name: Branmill Cove - Harbor Street
description: Harbor Street runs along the waterfront, connecting the Runedock to the Tidequay Market and the fishing quarter beyond. The street is broad enough for two loaded carts to pass, its surface paved with flat lake-stones fitted tightly together. Lamp-posts at regular intervals burn fish-oil through the night, and benches set against the low sea-wall give off-duty dockhands a place to watch the lake. The Mithril Chains are visible where they enter the water, and children sometimes dare each other to climb the chain-stanchions.

name: Branmill Cove - East Quarter
description: The east quarter of Branmill Cove is a residential neighborhood of stone-and-timber houses with slate roofs, their gardens facing narrow lanes that run perpendicular to the lake. Hasura Elf residents keep window-boxes of flowering herbs, and the lanes here smell of rosemary and woodsmoke in the evening. A small school occupies a converted warehouse at the quarter's center, and the sound of children reciting the tidal calendar in unison drifts through its open windows on still mornings.

name: Branmill Cove - West Quarter
description: The west quarter hugs the base of the bluffs where the Kailian's old high-water mark has stained the stone a dark mineral grey. The houses here are older than elsewhere in the city, their walls thick and their windows small â€” built in the era when storms off the lake were worse and the high-water season flooded the lower lanes. A community well stands at the quarter's center, its stonework carved with the names of families who have drawn water here for five generations. The smell of good ale drifts from a taproom on the corner.

name: Branmill Cove - North Lane
description: North Lane angles away from the lake toward the higher ground where the city's wealthier residents built their homes. The lane is shaded by mature linden trees whose roots have buckled sections of the paving, and the houses behind their garden walls are larger and more decorated than those near the dock. A stone fountain at the lane's midpoint depicts a barge riding a chain in stylized relief, the water flowing from a pipe cut to look like a rune-anchor. At Tidemill Festival, this fountain is garlanded with flowers and grain.

name: Branmill Cove - Reeve's Walk
description: A broad, tree-lined promenade that connects the Concordant Hall quarter to the residential north, Reeve's Walk is where the citizens of Branmill Cove take their evening air and where informal political business has been conducted for generations. Carved stone benches bear dedications to past Reeves, and a bulletin board near the walk's center posts civic announcements, trade regulations, and festival schedules. In the days before the Deceivers' War, three of the notices still pinned here were posted by the previous generation of Reeves and left up as a reminder.

name: Branmill Cove - Temple Row
description: A short street lined with three modest temples: one to the Lady of the Sea, one to Kaile'a Mistress of Waves, and one to Cinvarin the Five Witnesses, all built within a generation of each other. The architecture of all three is similar â€” pale stone, arched windows, and iron door-rings shaped like waves or chains â€” reflecting a community that honors its new Ascended Gods without abandoning the old Elemental Lords. A shared garden between the temples is tended by a rotating roster of novices, its beds planted with lake-iris and salt-grass.

name: Branmill Cove - Grain Market
description: A secondary market north of the main Tidequay Plaza, the Grain Market specializes in the processed products of the tidal harvest: flour, dried grain, barley malt, and the pressed oil extracted from lake-side seed plants. The smell is warm and dusty, and the scales here are certified by the Reeve Council every season. Eruskan Human merchants dominate this market, their families having worked the tidal fields since the Concordant era. Several have posted their Tide Turn harvest pledges on a board, a public commitment that holds real social weight.

name: Branmill Cove - Aleworks
description: The aleworks district sits on the city's northeast edge where the prevailing wind carries its yeasty smell away from the residential quarters. Three breweries operate here, their copper kettles and fermentation vats visible through open warehouse doors. Branmill Cove's lake-barley ale is exported westward through Kinsweave, and the head brewer of the largest brewery is a Halfling woman whose family has held the recipe for six generations. Empty barrels stamped with the Branmill mark wait in stacks outside, ready for the next caravan departure.

name: Branmill Cove - Smiths' Quarter
description: The ring of forges and workshops that occupies the city's northeast corner produces the iron fittings, chain-links, and rune-maintenance tools that keep the Mithril Chain system functional. The Pekakarlik smiths here are not the Giants who forged the original chains â€” no living smith could replicate that work â€” but they are expert maintainers, and their repair-links and anchor-brackets keep the ancient system running. A forge-master's board outside the main workshop lists the day's jobs and the estimated completion times.

name: Branmill Cove - Market Square Annex
description: An overflow market space on the Tidequay Market's western side, the Annex handles the seasonal glut of goods that won't fit the main plaza. At peak Tide Turn, this space is so densely packed with stalls that movement requires patience and elbow-work. At other times it is quieter, hosting a twice-weekly livestock market and a daily gathering of traveling merchants who cannot afford permanent stall space. The Reeve Council taxes the Annex at a lower rate than the main market, making it a proving ground for new traders.

name: Branmill Cove - Lakeside Steps
description: Wide stone steps cut into the lakeside bluff descend from Harbor Street to the water's edge, where the Kailian laps at the stone with a soft, steady sound. Fishing boats tie up at iron rings set into the steps, and at low water the dark silt of the tidal flats is visible below, already marked with the groove-lines left by chain-guided barges. The steps are old â€” Giant-old, some say, though they have been repaired and extended by every generation since. At dawn and dusk they are a gathering place for those who want to watch the light on the water.

name: Branmill Cove - Halfling Quarter
description: A cluster of lower-ceilinged buildings near the market's western edge, the Halfling Quarter is the most cheerfully decorated neighborhood in Branmill Cove. Window boxes overflow with bright flowers, and the lane is strung with colored paper lanterns that sway in the lake breeze. The tavern here â€” the Tidemill Arms â€” is famous for its pie and its noise, and the sound of singing on festival nights carries across half the city. Halfling children race through the lanes with the confidence of people who have never worried much about the height of a doorframe.

name: Tidemill Arms Tavern
description: The Tidemill Arms is everything a waterfront tavern ought to be: loud, warm, slightly too crowded, and smelling of excellent food. Low beams force taller patrons to duck, and the tables are scarred by generations of drink-rings and knife-games. A painted mural of the first Tidemill Festival covers the back wall, showing Halflings and Dwarves dancing on a grain-covered barge while the Mithril Chains gleam in painted moonlight. The innkeeper, a broad-shouldered Halfling woman, runs the room with cheerful authority and an excellent memory for who still owes a tab.

name: Tidemill Arms - Common Room Loft
description: The loft above the Tidemill Arms' main room is where traveling merchants and off-duty sailors sleep when the weather is bad or the rooms are full. Straw pallets are lined up along the slanted walls, and pegs for hanging gear are spaced every few feet. A small window faces the lake, and on clear nights the distant glow of the Runedock lanterns is visible. The loft is not comfortable exactly, but it is dry, and the noise from below fades after the second hour of ale.

name: Branmill Cove - Chapel of the Concordant
description: A modest civic chapel maintained by the Reeve Council, the Chapel of the Concordant serves those who hold to no specific faith but honor the idea of unity that the Eke Concordant once represented. Simple wooden benches face an altar decorated with four elemental symbols and the chain-link device of the Concordant. The chapel is open at all hours, and it is not unusual to find citizens here at midnight, sitting in the dark with the lamp burning low, working through problems that have no easy answer.

name: Branmill Cove - Merchant Row
description: A street of permanent shops facing the Tidequay Market's north side, Merchant Row is where Branmill Cove's most established traders keep their premises. Goods from across the Kailian region are available here: wind-silk from Tiravel, woodwind instruments from Myruvane, river-spears from Darnavar, and the Riverstone Amulets that are the region's most recognized export. The shopfronts are well-maintained, their signs freshly painted each spring, and the merchants who own them have council connections that keep their licenses current.

name: Branmill Cove - Amulet Workshop
description: The workshop where Branmill Cove's Riverstone Amulets are crafted occupies a converted boathouse near the Runedock. A Pekakarlik artisan and two Eruskan apprentices work at stone-cutting and rune-inscription benches, shaping the smooth lake-pebbles into amulets and pressing them with the elemental rune-patterns that give them their minor magical properties. The floor is covered in stone-dust and the air smells of mineral oil. Finished amulets dry on wooden racks before being packaged for export, and the best pieces â€” those with particularly clear rune-inscription â€” are set aside for direct sale.

name: Branmill Cove - Cartographer's Office
description: A narrow but well-organized office maintained by the Reeve Council's official cartographer, this space holds copies of every map of the Kailian chain-fields, tidal approaches, and regional trade routes produced in Branmill Cove's history. The cartographer, an elderly Hasura Elf with ink-stained fingers, charges a modest fee for copies and a higher one for updates reflecting recent chain-anchor shifts. The oldest map on the wall, framed behind glass, shows four more chain-anchor points than are currently in service â€” the ones sabotaged or lost in the Deceivers' War.

name: Branmill Cove - Council Courtyard
description: The open courtyard behind Concordant Hall is used for public assemblies when the chamber inside is too small for the crowd. A raised speaking-stone at the courtyard's center is worn smooth where generations of citizens and Reeves have stood to address the gathered community. The courtyard is also used for the public posting of Reeve decisions, trade regulations, and tax notices, and on Tidemill Festival the space is cleared for dancing that goes until the fish-oil lamps run dry. Ivy covers the hall's rear wall, its tendrils framing a carved relief of the original nine Concordant settlements.

name: Branmill Cove - Healer's Hall
description: Branmill Cove's healers maintain a combined clinic and apothecary in a long building adjacent to the Concordant Hall courtyard. The front room handles day-to-day injuries and illness; the back, screened by a heavy curtain, is used for surgical work and recovery. The healers are a mixed group â€” a Hasura Elf herbalist, two Eruskan Human clerics, and a Pekakarlik physic whose specialty is treating the crush-injuries common to dock work. The scent of medicinal herbs and clean linen pervades the space.

name: Branmill Cove - Armorer's Lane
description: A short lane between the Healer's Hall and the city wall where three armorers and a weaponsmith keep their shops. The work here is practical rather than ornate â€” ring-mail and scale suits for dock-guards and chain-watchmen, with spare capacity for adventurers who need a quick repair. A sign in the weaponsmith's window reads 'River Spears â€” Commissioned from Darnavar, inquire within,' and a crate of chain-maintenance tools sits ready for collection by the Runedock.

name: Branmill Cove - City Gate - North
description: The north gate of Branmill Cove is built into a section of old wall that predates the current city by at least two centuries, its stones larger and more crudely fitted than the dressed work around it. A guard post on each side is staffed by two civic guards who check the travel-tokens of those entering and log departures in a road-book. The gate road leads north toward the Whispering Tidewoods and, eventually, to the approaches of the Ashen Hollows beyond.

name: Branmill Cove - City Gate - East
description: The east gate faces the trade road that connects Branmill Cove to Velathenor along the southern Kailian shore. The gate is busier than the north, and a small customs shed beside it is staffed by a Reeve-appointed assessor who levies the incoming goods tax. Outbound caravans check out here, their manifests stamped with the Branmill seal. The view east along the road shows the first patches of Whispering Tidewood canopy, the forest's humid breath visible as a faint haze in warm weather.

name: Branmill Cove - Dockmaster's Office
description: A two-room building at the Runedock's landward end, the Dockmaster's office handles berth assignments, chain-maintenance scheduling, and the barge-use permits that every planting-season operator must hold. The Dockmaster is a practical Eruskan Human in her fifties, a former barge operator who ran the chain-fields for fifteen years before taking the desk job. Her wall holds a tide calendar of the next two years and a list of every chain-anchor point with its last inspection date marked in red for overdue and black for current.

name: Branmill Cove - Chain-Keeper's Lodge
description: The residence and working quarters of the senior Pekakarlik chain-keeper, a hereditary position that has passed through the Splitlock family for four generations. The lodge smells of metal polish and rune-chalk, and its shelves hold hand-copied diagrams of every chain-anchor in the Branmill section of the Mithril Chain system. Corvin Splitlock, the current keeper, is a compact Dwarf with callused hands and a permanent squint from working in close light. He does not welcome interruptions, but those who arrive with a genuine chain-maintenance problem find him generous with his time.

name: Branmill Cove - Tidal Fields Overlook
description: A raised embankment at the city's southern edge overlooks the tidal fields â€” the exposed Kailian seabed where the harvest is sown each season. At low water, the dark silt is furrowed in long parallel rows by the barge-mounted plows guided along the Mithril Chains, and the green of early grain shoots is visible from here when the season advances. At high water, the same ground is invisible beneath the lake, and the barges sit above it like patient birds. The Tide Turn ceremony is observed from this embankment by the whole city.

name: Branmill Cove - South Shore Path
description: A footpath along the lake's southern shore connects the Tidal Fields Overlook to the Runedock via the lakeside steps, passing through a stretch of reed-beds where the chain-anchor foundations are visible at low water. The path is narrow and sometimes muddy, and the reeds screen it from the city above, making it a favored route for those who want to move without being observed. The Mithril Chains pass directly overhead at one point, and on windy days the great links shift and hum with a low resonance that seems to come from the rune-inscriptions themselves.

name: Branmill Cove - Guild Hall
description: The Merchant and Craft Guild of Branmill Cove occupies a substantial stone building set between Merchant Row and the Council Courtyard. The guildhall serves as a dispute-resolution center, a contract registry, and a gathering space for the city's organized trades. Membership plaques for every guild branch line the entrance hall, and a locked room at the back holds the guild's arbitration records going back to the Concordant era. The Guild-master, a Halfling named Perrin Saltworth, is elected by member vote every three years and has held the position for nine.

name: Branmill Cove - Fishmonger's Wharf
description: The fishmonger's wharf extends from the lakeside steps eastward, its stone surface perpetually slick with water and fish-scales. The morning catch is unloaded here before dawn, sorted on long gutting tables, and either salted for export, sold fresh to market-buyers, or sent to the smoke-houses on the east shore. The smell is powerful and honest, and the gulls that follow the fishing boats inland wheel overhead in screaming competition. A posted price-board shows the day's fish rates by species, updated as each boat comes in.

name: Branmill Cove - Smoke-Houses
description: Three long smoke-houses on the city's eastern shore process the surplus fish catch for export and long-term storage. The buildings are low, windowless, and constantly wreathed in aromatic smoke from the alder-wood fires inside. Fish hang on iron hooks in long rows, their preservation a skilled trade that the Eruskan families who run these operations have refined over generations. The smoke-masters are identifiable by their grey-tinged hair and clothing; no amount of washing fully removes the alder scent from skin or fabric.

name: Branmill Cove - East Shore Road
description: A road running along the city's eastern edge connects the smoke-houses and fishers' quarter to the East Gate and the trade road beyond. Cart-ruts are cut deep into the surface where loaded fish-barrels roll to market each morning, and the road is wide enough here to allow loaded carts to turn. A row of Eruskan Human houses faces the road, their backyard fences bordering the reed-beds at the water's edge. Children fish from the fence-posts with improvised rods, pulling out the small silver fish that gather in the reed-shallows.

name: Branmill Cove - Hasura Quarter
description: The Hasura Elf community of Branmill Cove occupies a cluster of taller buildings near the city center, their upper floors connected by wooden bridges that allow movement between residences without descending to street level. Wind-chimes hang from every balcony, and the faint scent of wind-silk dye â€” a sweet, resinous smell â€” drifts from the upper workshops. The Hasura here are thirty percent of the city's population, descendants of those who stayed after the Concordant era rather than retreating to the forest, and they are woven as tightly into Branmill Cove's civic life as any Eruskan or Halfling.

name: Branmill Cove - Pekakarlik Quarter
description: The Pekakarlik Dwarf community clusters near the Runedock, their low-ceilinged residences and workshops forming a dense, practical neighborhood where everything is organized for function first. Workshop doors open directly onto the lane, and the sound of hammering and rune-chalk grinding is constant during working hours. The dwarves here are the chain-keepers, the forge-workers, and the precision craftsmen without whom the Mithril Chain system would fail, and they know their worth. A community meeting-house at the quarter's center doubles as a shrine to Gonmareck Ritler.

name: Branmill Cove - Harbor Overlook
description: A wide stone platform jutting from the blufftop above the Runedock provides the best view of the full chain-field operation. From here, the Mithril Chains can be traced from their anchor-posts on the dock down into the water and, at low tide, across the exposed seabed to the distant barges. During planting season the view is spectacular: a dozen flat-hulled barges moving in slow formation along the chain-lines, their crews small figures against the dark silt. The platform's railing is worn smooth where generations of residents have stood watching the tide-work that feeds the region.

name: Chaindock Plaza
description: The heart of Velathenor's trade is this wide stone plaza where the Mithril Chains anchor their western barges. The chains here are older than those at Branmill Cove, their links thicker and more deeply inscribed with elemental runes, and the anchor-posts are set into foundations that may be Giant-era work. Fish, grain, Riverstone Amulets, and trade-cloth change hands at the ring of stalls that lines the plaza. The smell of the lake is strong here â€” damp stone, fresh water, and the faint mineral scent of the rune-anchors doing their slow, patient work.

name: Seaspray Shrine
description: The Seaspray Shrine is built on a low platform above the tideline where spray from the Kailian's storms reaches the stone walls and leaves white salt-lines at varying heights, each one recording a different season's high water. The shrine is dedicated to the Lady of the Sea, and water magic cast here is amplified â€” the air hums faintly when a caster draws on water-elemental energy. A smaller alcove on the shrine's north side is dedicated to Kaile'a, Mistress of Waves, added since the Deceivers' War. The two devotions coexist without friction; the Lady of the Sea created the water, and Kaile'a walks upon it.

name: Velathenor - East Market Lane
description: A lane of permanent market stalls extending east from the Chaindock Plaza, this stretch specializes in the net and rope trade that keeps the fishing fleet operational. Pekakarlik rope-workers and Halfling net-menders share the lane, their complementary crafts making this a natural pairing. Spools of lake-reed fiber and imported hemp crowd the stalls, and finished nets in various mesh sizes hang overhead like geometric foliage. A battered scale hangs from a post at the lane's center for settling disputes about weight.

name: Velathenor - South Dock
description: The south dock of Velathenor faces the Kailian's calmer waters and handles the fishing fleet rather than the chain-barges. Smaller boats are drawn up on a graveled beach beside the dock, their hulls patched and repainted in the bright colors that Halfling fishers favor. The dock itself is timber-built, replaced section by section as the lake's water levels shift, and it creaks companionably underfoot. A fish-cleaning station at the dock's end is always in use during the morning hours, seagulls wheeling overhead in permanent competition.

name: Stiltmarket
description: The Stiltmarket extends over the tideline on a network of wooden piles driven into the Kailian's shallow margin, the stalls built on platforms that rise and fall with the lake's seasonal cycle. The structure creaks and shifts underfoot as the water moves beneath it, and the sound of water lapping against the piles is constant. Halfling vendors run most of the stalls, selling fresh fish, woven-reed baskets, lake pearls, and the small dried flower bundles that are a Velathenor specialty. At the market's center, a modest hearth-shrine to Semyon the Hearth Mother is decorated with the day's first and last fish of the catch.

name: Velathenor - Shrine Courtyard
description: The small courtyard behind the Seaspray Shrine is used for the water-blessing ceremonies held at the start of each fishing season and at Tide Turn. A stone basin at the courtyard's center is fed by a pipe that draws directly from the Kailian, and the water in it is always cold and clear regardless of the season. Offerings left at the basin â€” small stones, dried flowers, scales from the first catch â€” accumulate until the novices clean them away each week and return them to the lake. The courtyard's walls are carved with wave-patterns and the names of fishing families who have offered here for generations.

name: Velathenor - Pearl Traders' Row
description: A short lane connecting the east market to the Wavewatch Spire road, Pearl Traders' Row is where Velathenor's lake-pearl dealers keep their booths. Lake pearls from the Kailian are smaller and less uniform than sea pearls, but they carry a distinctive blue-grey luster that commands good prices in Kinsweave and the Twin Rivers. The traders here are mostly Halflings with one or two Eruskan Human dealers who moved in after the Deceivers' War. The security arrangements are visible â€” iron bars on the booth windows and a hired guard at the lane's entrance.

name: Velathenor - Fisher's Quarter
description: The Halfling majority of Velathenor is most visible in the Fisher's Quarter, where the houses are scaled to shorter residents and the lanes are friendly and communicative rather than direct. Children dart between the houses and through the narrow gaps in garden fences, and the smell of cooking fish and dried herbs drifts from every window. The community here maintained Velathenor's fishing operation through the worst years after the Deceivers' War, when the city's population swelled with refugees from the burned settlements and every boat had to work twice its normal range.

name: Velathenor - Stiltmarket Back Quay
description: The rear of the Stiltmarket faces deeper water where the chain-barges are moored between seasons. The barges here are Velathenor's rather than Branmill Cove's â€” slightly smaller, built for the shallower waters of the western Kailian â€” and their flat hulls bear the wave-chevron mark of Velathenor's barge-masters. A repair crane serves both the Stiltmarket's structure and the barges, and a workshop on the quay's edge smells of caulking compound and fresh timber. Mayor Corda Whitewave is known to walk this quay in the early morning, checking the chain-barges' condition before the city wakes.

name: Velathenor - Mayor's Hall
description: Mayor Corda Whitewave governs Velathenor from a modest hall near the Seaspray Shrine, its entrance marked by a carved lintel showing a navigation chart of the Kailian chain-fields. The hall is functional rather than grand â€” Velathenor's civic ambitions run to keeping the barges seaworthy and the catch profitable, not to architectural display. Whitewave's office is off the main hall, its walls covered in navigational charts, trade ledgers, and the list of ship losses from the Deceivers' War that the Mayor keeps posted as a reminder of the cost of complacency.

name: Wavewatch Spire - Base
description: The stone base of the Wavewatch Spire is broader than Branmill Cove's Seawind Spire, reflecting the greater exposure of Velathenor's position on the Kailian's western shore where storm-fetch can be severe. Iron shutters on the base windows can be closed and bolted when lake storms drive spray inland, and the door is set back in a recessed arch that breaks the wind. A plaque beside the door lists the names of four watchmen who died during the Deceivers' War holding their posts to give the city time to evacuate civilians.

name: Velathenor - Barge-Master's Quarter
description: The barge-masters who operate Velathenor's chain-barge fleet live in a cluster of solid houses near the Fisher's Quarter, their yards equipped with the models and plans used for training new crew. The barge-masters are a close-knit group â€” most have family connections spanning three or four generations of the chain-field operation â€” and their informal authority over the planting season operation rivals the Mayor's civic authority on matters relating to the lake. A model barge in the quarter's common yard is used to demonstrate chain-guide procedures to apprentice crews.

name: Velathenor - Baysong Festival Ground
description: A wide, flat space near the north dock is reserved year-round for the Baysong Festival, Velathenor's greatest civic event. At festival time the ground is covered with temporary stages, lantern-posts, and the mooring stakes for the decorated parade-barges that process along the Kailian shore. During the festival, the Seaspray Shrine's blessing ceremony is performed here under open sky, with the Mayor leading the city in the traditional Tide-Call that marks the start of the planting season. The rest of the year the space serves as a practice field for the barge-crews and a gathering place for the Fisher's Quarter community.

name: Velathenor - Trade Registrar's Office
description: The trade registrar handles the documentation that Velathenor's merchants depend on: cargo manifests, chain-field use permits, export certifications, and the disputed-goods ledger that records every trade disagreement brought before the Mayor's office in the past two decades. The registrar is an Eruskan Human with a clerk and two assistants, and their work is the administrative backbone of a city whose entire economy moves with the tidal calendar. A board outside the office posts the current week's barge-schedule and the registered cargo manifests for any vessel using the chain-field.

name: Wavewatch Spire - Tower
description: From the tower of the Wavewatch Spire, the full western Kailian shore is visible in both directions, and in clear weather the Stonewind Plains are a brown-gold line to the southwest. Storm-watch is the primary duty here â€” the Kailian's high-water storms can raise waves that threaten the Stiltmarket and the lower docks, and three hours' warning is what the watch is meant to provide. A signal-mirror on the east parapet communicates with Branmill Cove's Seawind Spire. The watchman currently on duty is tracking a bank of dark cloud forming over the northern lake.

name: Velathenor - North Road
description: The north road of Velathenor leads away from the lake into the lower margin of the Whispering Tidewoods, its surface changing from lake-stone paving to packed earth within a hundred yards of the city gate. The trees close in quickly â€” the Tidewoods are dense and humid, and the canopy begins before the road has left the city's outer buildings behind. Signs nailed to trees mark the path to Tiravel and warn of the approaches to the Ashen Hollows further north. The road is patrolled twice daily by a pair of civic guards who check for Kin-sense anomalies along the first mile.

name: Velathenor - Rope-Walk
description: A long, narrow building where the heaviest ropes used by the chain-barge fleet are laid and twisted, the rope-walk runs parallel to the north dock. The building is always occupied during working hours by teams walking backward while twisting fiber strands â€” the traditional method that produces rope with superior breaking strength. The floor is covered in loose fiber and the air is dense with plant-dust. Finished rope is coiled in great loops against the walls, waiting for inspection and sale. The rope-walk also produces the hawsers used to secure the Stiltmarket to its pile-foundations during high-water storms.

name: Velathenor - Halfling Enclave
description: The densest concentration of Halfling residents in Velathenor occupies a warren of lanes near the trade registrar's office, their houses smaller and more cheerfully decorated than Eruskan structures nearby. Painted door-frames, window-boxes, and garden trellises covered in climbing flowers make the enclave the most colorful quarter of the city. The community well in the enclave's center is surrounded by benches where older residents sit and talk through the afternoon, and the smell of baking bread and drying fish is omnipresent. This community's ancestors hosted the riverside feasts of the Concordant era, and that tradition of generosity has not been lost.

name: Velathenor - East Gate
description: The east gate of Velathenor opens onto the lake-shore road to Branmill Cove, a well-maintained route that hugs the Kailian's southern margin for most of its length. The gate itself is modest â€” a pair of timber doors set in a low stone wall â€” but the guardhouse is solidly built and permanently staffed. A board by the gate posts the current tidal level and forecast, since travelers caught on the low-shore road at high water can find sections flooded. The duty guard notes departures and arrivals and reports anything unusual to the Wavewatch every evening.

name: Velathenor - Healers' Cottage
description: A small but well-equipped healer's cottage near the shrine courtyard serves Velathenor's medical needs. The healer, a Halfling woman trained in both conventional medicine and water-elemental remedies, treats the cuts, infections, and waterborne illnesses common to a lake-shore community. Her herb garden behind the cottage is organized with the precision of someone who has treated every type of injury the Kailian can produce, and her cabinet of prepared remedies is rarely less than full. The cottage has a second room for patients who need overnight rest, its window facing the lake.

name: Velathenor - Merchant Hostel
description: A clean, functional hostel for merchants visiting Velathenor on trade business, the Merchant Hostel occupies a converted warehouse near the Chaindock Plaza. Private rooms with locking doors are available by the night or the tenday, and the common dining room serves simple food at reasonable prices. The hostel-keeper maintains a message board where merchants can post buying and selling notices, and a locked strong-room is available for valuables by arrangement. Most guests are chain-barge operators or grain-traders, and the dinner conversation is primarily about tidal forecasts and Riverstone Amulet prices.

name: Velathenor - Net-Menders' Hall
description: A cooperative workspace where the city's net-menders work on commission repairs and their own stock production, the Net-Menders' Hall is a social as much as an economic institution. Large tables allow multiple workers to spread nets for repair work, and the hall is almost always occupied, with workers of varying ages sharing techniques and gossip in equal measure. The hall maintains a lending library of net-patterns and a record of the fishing grounds' current conditions contributed by the skippers who bring in repair work. The oldest pattern in the library dates to three generations before the Deceivers' War.

name: Velathenor - South Shore
description: The southern shore of Velathenor's lakefront is quieter than the Stiltmarket side, its reed-beds thick and undisturbed except where the path to the Tidal Fields approaches. Herons stand motionless in the shallows, and the cries of waterfowl are constant. At low tide, the dark silt of the chain-field furrows is visible here, and the nutrient-rich smell of exposed lake-bottom rises on warm days. A stone bench near the path's beginning is worn smooth; this is where the city's old fishers come to watch the tidal levels and predict the coming season's harvest.

name: Velathenor - Grain Storage
description: Three large stone granaries near the east gate hold Velathenor's tidal harvest surplus. The granaries are built with thick walls, ventilated floors, and iron doors to protect against moisture, vermin, and fire. A full-time granary-keeper and two assistants manage the stores, rotating stock to prevent spoilage and maintaining the records that tell the Mayor exactly how many months of reserve the city holds at any given time. The current reserve, posted on a board at the granary entrance, represents about eight months of city consumption â€” normal for this point in the tidal cycle.

name: Velathenor - Community Hall
description: Velathenor's community hall is a large, single-story building near the Mayor's Hall where town meetings, festivals, and community celebrations are held. The floor is polished timber, the ceiling beamed with lake-oak, and the walls hung with fishing nets, carved paddles, and a large painted map of the Kailian and its chain-fields. At Baysong Festival the hall is the center of the indoor celebrations, its floor cleared for dancing and its tables laden with the best of the harvest. The hall is also available for hire by families for celebrations, and is booked months in advance for the prime festival dates.

name: Velathenor - Outer Ward
description: The outer ward of Velathenor encompasses the area between the city wall and the first ring of buildings, a buffer zone where carts load and unload, livestock is temporarily penned, and travelers' camps are permitted within sight of the gate guards. The ward is never empty and rarely quiet â€” the commerce of a city of 6,500 generates constant traffic â€” and the smells shift as the wind changes between fish from the docks, grain from the market, and woodsmoke from the houses. A public well serves the outer ward, its stone trough also used as a livestock watering point.

name: Velathenor - Tidewoods Trail Head
description: At the northern edge of Velathenor, the city yields to the Whispering Tidewoods without ceremony. The last houses give way to garden plots, the garden plots give way to scrub, and the scrub to tall canopy within fifty yards. The trailhead here is marked with a carved post noting distances to Tiravel and cautioning travelers about the condition of the trail. Kin-sense should be maintained at all times beyond this point, the carving advises â€” a warning that would have seemed excessive before the Deceivers' War and now seems merely prudent.

name: Velathenor - Dye-House Lane
description: A short lane near the Fisher's Quarter where three dyers work with the lake-plant pigments that produce Velathenor's characteristic blue and green fabrics. Fabric hangs on lines between the buildings in various stages of drying, creating a shifting canopy of color above the lane. The dye-vats are outside when the weather permits â€” the smell is pungent enough to require it â€” and the dyers' hands and forearms are permanently stained to the elbow. The dyed fabric is sold to garment-makers throughout the region and is a minor but reliable export.

name: Velathenor - The Wavecrest Inn
description: The Wavecrest Inn is Velathenor's primary accommodation for travelers and visiting merchants, a rambling timber building near the Chaindock Plaza whose rooms look out over the lake. The inn was named for Captain Marin Wavecrest, who covered Whistlebank's evacuation during the Deceivers' War with fog magic before a DÃ³mnathar spell sank her ship. A carved relief of a ship in fog marks the inn's sign, and the naming is both memorial and a statement of what this city remembers. The innkeeper serves a good table and keeps a record of all guests at the Mayor's request.

name: Velathenor - Pekakarlik Workshop
description: The Pekakarlik population of Velathenor is smaller than at Branmill Cove, but their workshop near the Runedock equivalent handles the precision work that no one else can do: chain-guide ring replacement, rune-anchor maintenance, and the manufacturing of the iron fittings that keep the Stiltmarket's pile-structure sound. The workshop is smaller than Branmill Cove's Smiths' Quarter but no less precise, and the head smith here has a collegial relationship with Corvin Splitlock at Branmill Cove, exchanging technical notes by barge every season.

name: Velathenor - Old Wall Walk
description: A narrow walkway along the top of Velathenor's oldest wall section provides a different perspective on the city and the lake. The wall here predates the current construction, its stones larger and cruder, and sections have been repaired so many times that the patchwork is its own history. The walk is used by guards on rounds and by residents who come here for the view and the wind, which is stronger along the wall-top than in the lanes below. On stormy days the lake spray reaches here, coating the stones with a fine salt-haze that dries white in the sun.

name: Velathenor - Barge-Crew Barracks
description: The barge-crew barracks house the seasonal workers who come to Velathenor for the planting and harvest periods but do not keep permanent residences in the city. The building is functional â€” dormitory-style rooms, a common kitchen, and a secure storage room for equipment â€” and the seasonal workers who use it are a mix of Eruskan Humans, Halflings, and occasional Pekakarlik Dwarves who have worked the chain-fields for years. The barracks' common room has a reputation for lively card-games in the evenings, and the barracks-master turns a blind eye to a reasonable amount of noise after dark.

name: Velathenor - Lake Road South
description: The southern continuation of Velathenor's main lake road leads toward the Sundrift Veil and eventually to the connections between Tidebloom Reach and Kinsweave. The road is paved for the first mile, then transitions to a maintained gravel track. Milestones â€” smooth lake-stones carved with distances and directional indicators â€” mark the route every quarter-mile. Two supply-depot posts along this road are maintained by Velathenor's civic authority for the use of travelers and official messengers.

name: Velathenor - Civic Garden
description: A formal garden beside the Mayor's Hall provides Velathenor with its only purely aesthetic public space. The garden is maintained by civic volunteers and planted with the lake-shore plants that are native to the Kailian margin: lake-iris, reed-grass, water-mint, and the small blue flowers that bloom at Tide Turn and are gathered for the Baysong Festival garlands. A fountain at the garden's center uses a pipe from the lake supply, and the sound of water running over carved stone is a constant gentle presence. Benches allow residents to sit in the garden, and the Mayor's Hall windows look out over it.

name: Velathenor - East Warehouse District
description: A cluster of warehouses near the east gate handles the bulk storage of imported goods â€” iron fittings from the Pekakarlik quarters, timber from the Tidewoods, and the trade goods arriving from Branmill Cove and beyond. The warehouses are owned by a Halfling consortium that charges storage fees and provides security, their guards equipped with the lake-iron weapons that Velathenor's smiths produce. The district is busy during the active trade season and quiet between Tide Turns when the chain-barges are out of service.

name: Velathenor - Shrine of the Wave-Martyr
description: A small outdoor shrine near the Baysong Festival ground commemorates the Kin who died when the Starwave â€” the great Halfling vessel â€” was sunk by Deceiver magic during the war. Names are carved into a low stone wall that curves around a shallow pool fed by the lake, and small boat-shaped offerings float on the pool's surface, set there by families who lost someone on the Starwave or in the broader chaos of the war's lake-phase. The pool is always clean; Velathenor's children learn early that disturbing the shrine is one of the few things that will unite the entire community against them.

name: Velathenor - Armorer and Supply
description: A combined armorer and general supply shop near the Fisher's Quarter serves the working needs of the city's residents and the occasional adventurer. The armorer's section handles light armor â€” padded, leather, and occasional ring-mail â€” along with the short, broad lake-knives that Halfling fishers favor. The supply section stocks rope, lantern oil, preserved food, and the small luxuries â€” dried fruit, quality thread, good paper â€” that a city of 6,500 generates steady demand for. The proprietor, an Eruskan Human woman, runs both sections without help and knows where everything is.

name: Velathenor - Lakeside Promenade
description: A stone-paved walk along the Kailian shore north of the Stiltmarket, the Lakeside Promenade is Velathenor's social gathering place. Residents walk here at dawn and dusk, watching the lake's light change and keeping informal watch on the chain-field conditions. Benches face the water at intervals, and the promenade's railing is hung with wind-bells whose sound carries across the city when the breeze picks up. At Baysong Festival the promenade is decorated with lanterns and flowers, and the parade-barges pass close enough to shore that the crews can throw flowers to the crowd.

name: Velathenor - Harbor Master's Office
description: The Harbor Master's Office coordinates all vessel traffic on Velathenor's section of the Kailian, from the chain-barges to the fishing fleet to the occasional trade-vessel from Branmill Cove. A large water-level gauge visible from the office window drives the scheduling decisions that determine when chain-barge operations are safe to conduct, and the Harbor Master's logs go back four generations, providing a detailed record of the lake's seasonal behavior that is invaluable for predicting flood and drought. The current Harbor Master is a Halfling man who inherited the position from his mother and grandmother before him.

name: Velathenor - Trade Road Junction
description: The main road through Velathenor meets the lake-shore road and the Tidewoods trail here at a three-way junction marked by a carved stone waypost. Distances to Branmill Cove, Tiravel, and the Stonewind Plains are inscribed on the post's faces, and the post itself is old enough that moss has grown into the carved lettering, making it easier to read than when it was first cut. A small shelter beside the junction provides rain cover for travelers waiting for companions or reading the waypost in foul weather.

name: Skyspire Vaults - Main Archive
description: The Skyspire Vaults occupy the upper chambers of Tiravel's tallest sky-oak, the archive accessible only by a series of rope bridges and climbing platforms that test the nerve of those unfamiliar with canopy travel. Inside, the humidity is carefully managed by Hasura Elf air-shaping techniques, keeping the wind-silk scrolls and vellum records in the precise conditions required for preservation. Lore-Keeper Sylara Windbloom moves through the stacks with the ease of someone born among these records, her fingers trailing across scroll-cases as she navigates by memory and touch. The oldest records here date to the first generation after the Eke Concordant's founding.

name: Skyspire Vaults - Entrance Platform
description: A broad wooden platform at canopy level serves as the entrance point to the Skyspire Vaults, its railings carved with the wind-scroll motifs that identify Hasura Elf craftwork. Starwind Blades guard the platform in pairs, their attention sweeping the canopy approaches and the forest floor far below. The platform sways slightly in the wind â€” a sensation that residents find comforting and visitors find alarming â€” and the view through the canopy gaps is one of the best in Tiravel: the Kailian's silver surface to the south and west, the darker mass of the Tidewoods to the north. A notice board posts current access permissions and research protocols.

name: Skyspire Vaults - Song Archive
description: A secondary archive chamber off the main Vaults, the Song Archive holds the wind-silk scrolls that record Hillmeet's songs â€” the musical tradition of the burned city, preserved by those Hasura Elves who escaped with their instruments and their memory. Lore-Keeper Windbloom has spent a decade reconstructing the full song-cycle of Hillmeet from fragments, oral accounts, and the partial scrolls recovered from the Ashen Hollows. A glass case near the center holds what she believes is a fragment of Aerith Windweave's notation â€” the last song the bard composed before the burning. Access requires the Lore-Keeper's personal authorization.

name: Tiravel - Canopy Lane
description: Tiravel exists on two levels: the forest floor and the canopy. Canopy Lane is the sky-oak platform network that connects the upper-level residences, workshops, and public spaces that the Hasura Elf majority inhabits by preference. Rope bridges link the platforms, their surfaces non-slip and their railings solid, and the lanes between the platforms are wide enough for two people to pass. The air at canopy level is cleaner and windier than on the forest floor, and the sound of wind-chimes from every platform creates a layered music that is the sound of Tiravel to those who know the city.

name: Windweave Market
description: Set on a broad platform in Tiravel's canopy, the Windweave Market is the principal trading floor for the wind-silk and woven-net exports that define the city's economy. Wind-silk comes in bolts and as finished garments, its surface catching light in ways that plain cloth does not. Wind Cloaks â€” the most prized item sold here, enchanted to add ten feet to the wearer's fly speed â€” are kept in a locked case behind the market-master's booth and sold only by appointment. The smell of fresh dye and sizing agent mingles with the forest air, and the wind across the platform keeps the hanging fabric in constant, gentle motion.

name: Tiravel - Weaving Hall
description: The largest platform in Tiravel's canopy network houses the Weaving Hall, where Hasura Elf weavers work the wind-silk looms that are the city's primary productive capacity. The looms themselves are sophisticated instruments, their frames made from sky-oak and their shuttle mechanisms guided by subtle air-shaping that allows the weavers to work at speeds and precision levels impossible with purely manual technique. The finished wind-silk has a characteristic translucent quality that comes from the air-infusion process, and fabric from Tiravel can be identified by a faint shimmer visible in direct light.

name: Canopy Watch Platform
description: The northernmost platform of Tiravel's canopy network serves as the Canopy Watch post, a surveillance station with the most direct view of the northern Tidewoods where the approaches to the Tomb of Kings and the Ashen Hollows lie. Na'wasua Starwind Blades staff this post at all times, their Kin-sense extended and active during their watches. The post's log records every anomaly reported in the northern forest, and the pattern of dead-zone sightings near the Tomb of Kings has accumulated over years into something that looks less like coincidence and more like a systematic presence. The log is reviewed by Lore-Keeper Windbloom monthly.

name: Tiravel - Sky-Oak Residences, East
description: The eastern cluster of residential platforms house the families of Tiravel's weavers and market-workers, their sky-oak chambers furnished with the spare elegance that Hasura Elves prefer. Each residence has a wind-garden â€” a small platform off the living space where wind-sensitive plants are grown in purpose-built containers that capture and redirect breezes. The gardens are also used for meditation and for the small personal ritual-markings that Hasura Elves observe privately. The eastern residences are newer than the western ones, rebuilt after the Deceivers' War to house Hillmeet's refugees.

name: Tiravel - Sky-Oak Residences, West
description: The western residential platforms are older than the eastern ones, their timbers weathered to a dark silver-grey that the Hasura consider a mark of quality. Some of these platforms were here when Tiravel was a small Hasura outpost before the Eke Concordant, and their construction techniques â€” mortise and tenon without iron hardware, knot-bindings that tighten under stress â€” represent a traditional approach that the newer platforms replaced with iron brackets and rope. The Hasura families who live here maintain the old techniques, repairing and replacing platform members using the same methods their ancestors used.

name: Starlit Glade
description: Below the canopy network, in a clearing where the sky-oaks stand back and the sky is visible in a broad circle, lies the Starlit Glade: Tiravel's ritual site and its most emotionally layered space. Air magic cast here is amplified, the gust of wind effect powerful enough to require a Fort save of DC 14 to resist. Since the Deceivers' War, the Glade has become a place of remembrance as much as ritual â€” stone markers for Hillmeet's dead stand among the roots, and offerings to Cinvarin and quiet prayers to Harreem are left in the shadows of the sky-oak boles. The Harreem prayers are never spoken aloud, but everyone knows they are there.

name: Tiravel - Forest Floor - Main Path
description: The main path at Tiravel's forest floor level connects the city's ground-level buildings and the base-stairs of the canopy platforms. Most of the city's non-Elf visitors and the Halfling minority who prefer ground-level living use this path for daily movement. The forest floor here is carpeted with orchid-laden undergrowth, the canopy above so thick that the light is permanently filtered green. The sound of the Tidewoods is ever-present: bird calls, the rustle of small animals in the undergrowth, and the creak of sky-oaks moving in the wind high above. The air is humid and smells of rich earth and growing things.

name: Tiravel - Ground-Level Market
description: A smaller market on the forest floor serves the ground-level population and handles bulk goods too heavy to move easily to the canopy. Grain, timber, rope, and iron fittings are traded here, along with the fresh-caught fish brought up from the Kailian shore by carriers who make the round trip twice daily. The market is smaller and louder than the Windweave Market above, and the traders here are more likely to be Halflings and the occasional Eruskan Human than the Hasura Elves who dominate the canopy economy. A cooperation between the two markets is essential and functional, if not always harmonious.

name: Tiravel - Halfling Quarter
description: The ground-level Halfling community in Tiravel is smaller and more recent than the Halfling presence in the lake-shore cities, most of its residents descendants of the refugees who came when Whistlebank and Mossgrove burned. Their houses are built against the bases of sky-oaks, using the great roots as partial walls â€” a practical adaptation that required some adjustment to Halfling building tradition but produced structures that are warm, dry, and remarkably solid. A communal kitchen serves the quarter, its fire never going out, and the smell of food cooking drifts north to the main path at most hours.

name: Tiravel - Lore-Keeper's Lodge
description: Lore-Keeper Sylara Windbloom maintains her personal residence and working space in a sky-oak chamber at the canopy's lower edge, accessible from both the canopy network and the forest floor by different stair-routes. The lodge is cluttered with the organized disorder of an active scholar â€” open scrolls, reference texts, ink bottles, and the long-handled quills preferred for wind-silk work. A current project on Windbloom's desk relates to the Kin-sense dead-zones near the Tomb of Kings, cross-referenced with reports from the Canopy Watch and the adventure hooks that her scouts have brought back. She receives visitors by appointment and is known to be direct, even when what she has to say is difficult.

name: Tiravel - Starwind Blade Barracks
description: The Na'wasua Starwind Blades who guard Tiravel's archives and watch the northern approaches maintain their barracks on the forest floor's eastern side. The barracks is spare and functional â€” weapon racks, training dummies, individual cells for rest â€” with the particular neatness of a military community that spends its alert time on the canopy platforms. Off-duty Blades practice in a cleared area outside the barracks, working through forms that combine blade-work with the air-shaping techniques that make the Na'wasua particularly effective forest fighters. Visitors are not discouraged, but idle socializing with the guards on duty is.

name: Tiravel - Forest Trail to Kailian Shore
description: A trail from Tiravel's southern edge descends through the Tidewoods toward the Kailian shore, used by the carriers who bring fish from the lake and by residents who want access to the water without using the main road. The trail is marked with Hasura way-signs â€” carved bark and bent branch configurations that are clear to anyone taught to read them and invisible to those who are not. The forest is thicker on this trail than on the main path, the canopy unbroken and the undergrowth requiring attention to navigate, but the trail is well-maintained and the distance to the shore is less than a mile.

name: Tiravel - East Gate and Road
description: Tiravel's east gate is little more than a marked gap in the low earthwork that delineates the city's boundary, the Tidewoods continuing on both sides without interruption. The gate is staffed by two Starwind Blades who check for unusual items and maintain the log of entrances. The road east leads toward the Stonewind Plains, where the trees thin and the sky opens up â€” a transition that Tiravel's Hasura Elf residents rarely make willingly, preferring the canopy's shelter to the open prairie. The road is used mainly by traders bringing iron and stone goods from the west and by the occasional party of adventurers heading toward the Ashen Hollows.

name: Tiravel - Wind-Silk Dye Works
description: On the forest floor below the Weaving Hall, the dye works processes the plant and mineral pigments that give Tiravel's wind-silk its distinctive colors. The dyeing process takes advantage of the Tidewoods' native plants â€” lake-iris extract for blue, bark-tannin for grey, and a complex preparation of forest mushroom for the deep green that is most associated with Tiravel's exports. The dye-master, a Hasura Elf who trained at both Tiravel and the lost Foxfen grove, uses techniques inherited from both communities. The smell of the dye works is sharp and distinctive, detectable from the canopy above.

name: Tiravel - Hillmeet Memorial
description: A small memorial grove at Tiravel's northern edge honors the Hasura Elves of Hillmeet. The grove is planted with sky-oak saplings â€” descendants of seeds salvaged from Hillmeet's burned canopy â€” and each sapling bears a small carved marker with a name from the lost city. Lord Pryce Skysong's name is not among them: Tiravel does not honor the king whose arrogance cost his people everything, but neither is he formally condemned here. The grove is a place for grief, not politics. Bard Aerith Windweave's name is carved on the largest stone, and fresh flowers are almost always present.

name: Tiravel - Healer's Treehouse
description: A healer's facility built into the lower canopy of a large sky-oak provides medical services to both the canopy and forest-floor populations. The healer is a Hasura Elf with training in both conventional healing and the wind-elemental remedies that are Tiravel's medical specialty â€” preparations that use the amplified air magic of the Starlit Glade to enhance recovery times and reduce fever. The treehouse clinic has six patient berths, a preparation room, and a herb-drying space on an outdoor platform. Hillmeet refugee survivors gave this clinic its endowment, and it has operated without interruption since the first refugees arrived.

name: Tiravel - Apprentice Weavers' Hall
description: Young weavers learning the wind-silk craft train in a dedicated hall below the main Weaving Hall, its looms simpler and its atmosphere more forgiving of mistakes. Instruction is provided by senior weavers on a rotating basis, and the training pieces produced here â€” technically wind-silk but lacking the precise air-infusion of master work â€” are sold at reduced prices in the ground-floor market. The hall also teaches the net-weaving techniques that are Tiravel's secondary export, the patterns learned from Whistlebank's surviving weavers integrated into the curriculum after the Deceivers' War.

name: Tiravel - Singing Platform
description: A platform in the canopy reserved for the musical traditions that are central to Hasura Elf culture. The Singing Platform is where Lore-Keeper Windbloom teaches Hillmeet's reconstructed song-cycle, where the Starweave Festival's performance events take place, and where individual musicians come to practice with the benefit of the canopy's natural acoustics. Wind instruments respond differently at this height â€” the ambient air-elemental energy adds harmonics that cannot be reproduced at ground level â€” and players who learn their instruments here sometimes struggle to adjust to the comparative flatness of indoor performance.

name: Tiravel - Tomb Road Patrol Station
description: A small garrison building near Tiravel's northern edge, the Tomb Road Patrol Station houses the rotating watch that monitors the forest road to the Tomb of Kings. Four Starwind Blades are stationed here at all times, and the patrol rota is posted inside the door alongside the standing orders regarding Kin-sense anomaly reporting. The station log records every patrol report going back three years, and the accumulation of dead-zone sightings in the Tomb's vicinity has become a matter of quiet concern that is discussed at patrol briefings but not publicly. The door is always unlocked and the tea always hot.

name: Tiravel - Wind-Reader's Post
description: A tall platform at canopy-top where Tiravel's wind-readers â€” Hasura Elves trained to interpret the elemental messages carried in the Tidewoods' air currents â€” maintain their observation. The post is exposed to the full force of whatever wind moves through the forest, and the wind-readers have developed a physical toughness to match their metaphysical sensitivity. Their reports inform the agricultural planning for the chain-fields below, the storm-watch for the Kailian shore, and the monitoring of the North Wind for the Kin-sense fluctuations that sometimes precede disturbances near the Tomb. A set of bronze wind-tools hangs from the post's rail, their functions decipherable only to the trained.

name: Tiravel - South Road
description: The road south from Tiravel leads to the Kailian shore and eventually connects to the routes between Velathenor and Branmill Cove. The Tidewoods thin gradually as the lake approaches, the canopy lowering and the undergrowth opening into the reed-beds and tidal flats of the shore. Tiravel's residents use this road for the fish-carrying trade and for the occasional journey to the lake-shore cities, though most Hasura Elves prefer the forest trails and the canopy connections to the open road. The first mile is marked with Hasura way-signs; beyond that, regular carved posts serve travelers of all cultures.

name: Tiravel - Na'wasua Quarter
description: The fifteen percent of Tiravel's population that is Na'wasua Elf lives in a distinct but integrated quarter of the canopy, their platforms slightly higher than the Hasura residential cluster and connected by a separate but linked bridge network. Na'wasua architecture favors clean lines and minimal decoration, a contrast to the Hasura preference for carved detail. The Starwind Blades who guard the Vaults and the Watch platform are mostly drawn from this quarter, and the training forms they practice in the early morning are visible from the platforms below. Relations between Hasura and Na'wasua in Tiravel are respectful and close, shaped by shared loss.

name: Tiravel - Spellinkers' Hall
description: A small hall where Tiravel's spell-ink trade is organized and conducted, the Spellinkers' Hall connects the city's scholarly production with its mercantile needs. Spell-ink â€” prepared from Tidewoods plants using techniques requiring air-elemental attunement â€” is one of Tiravel's more valuable exports, used in enchantment work throughout Tidebloom Reach and traded west to Kinsweave. The hall maintains quality standards for ink production and handles export contracts. A display case holds examples of the best current production, and buyers can order custom blends by specification.

name: Tiravel - Community Fire Pit
description: At the base of the largest sky-oak in Tiravel's center, an ancient fire pit provides the ground-level equivalent of the community gathering that happens on the canopy. The fire here is kept burning from the first cold night of autumn to the last of spring, and it serves as a gathering point for the forest-floor community â€” Halflings, non-resident visitors, and those Hasura who simply prefer the ground on a given evening. Stories told around this fire are expected to be true or acknowledged as legend; Tiravel's cultural tradition does not distinguish formally between the two categories, only between the honest teller and the dishonest one.

name: Tiravel - Storage and Provisions
description: A network of covered storage structures at the forest floor handles the bulk goods that Tiravel's canopy-based economy requires: food stores, workshop supplies, the imported iron and stone that sky-oak construction needs for its fittings. The provisioner who manages this network is a Halfling with a dry sense of humor and an exact knowledge of every item in stock, and she guards the storage master-key with the same vigilance that the Starwind Blades bring to the Skyspire Vaults. Access to the secured stores requires authorization and is logged; the unsecured area is open to residents during daylight.

name: Tiravel - Tidewoods Trailhead - North
description: The northern trailhead of Tiravel opens onto the deeper Tidewoods and the forest roads that lead eventually to the Tomb of Kings and the ruins of Hillmeet. The trail is marked with Starwind Blade patrol signs and warning indicators that change based on the current security assessment. Today the signs indicate caution â€” the Canopy Watch has logged two Kin-sense anomaly reports in the northern sector this week, both in the same approximate location. Travelers heading north are expected to register at the Tomb Road Patrol Station. The forest is quiet here, the Tidewoods' sounds normal, but the quiet has a watchful quality that experienced travelers learn to respect.

name: Silksong Aviary
description: The Silksong Aviary is Myruvane's most celebrated landmark, a great dome of living sky-oak branches woven together over three generations into a seamless vault. Inside, thousands of silk-moths circulate through a controlled environment of temperature, humidity, and carefully managed air-elemental currents. At festival time the Aviary blooms with bioluminescence â€” the moths' natural response to the ceremonial chants that the Hasura Elf attendants perform, their bodies producing a soft amber-and-blue light that transforms the interior into something between a forest and a dream. The silk the moths produce here is finer than standard wind-silk, and its production is Myruvane's most closely guarded craft secret.

name: Myruvane - Aviary Gardens
description: The gardens surrounding the Silksong Aviary supply the food plants and temperature-regulating vegetation that keep the moth population healthy. Pasua Elf gardeners tend these beds with a combination of conventional horticulture and the gentle nature-shaping that is their particular skill, encouraging a complex micro-ecosystem that has been in balance since the aviary was first established. The smell of the gardens is rich and complicated â€” flower pollen, leaf-mold, and the slightly sweet exhalation of the moths that drift from the aviary's ventilation openings. Visitors are welcome in the outer gardens but directed away from the moth-habitat sections.

name: Melody Hollow
description: An oak-walled chamber at the base of a great sky-oak, Melody Hollow is both a workshop and a cultural heart. Instrument-makers â€” primarily flute-crafters â€” work here by day, their pieces shaped from the sky-oak timber that gives Myruvane's instruments their distinctive warm tone. Air magic cast within Melody Hollow is amplified; sound burst effects are noticeably more powerful here, and performers training in the Hollow develop a sensitivity to harmonic nuance that stays with them in performance elsewhere. Harreem's influence is quietly acknowledged here: bards who craft instruments in the Hollow sometimes say that the Golden Rose was a musician first, and that her rage began as grief. They do not say it loudly.

name: Tideweave Bridge
description: The Tideweave Bridge is Myruvane's engineering marvel â€” a span of woven silk-rope and sky-oak planks stretching across the inlet where the Kailian's water reaches deepest into the forest, connecting the canopy platforms of the south district to the barge-landing below. The silk weave of the bridge is not merely structural: it is enchanted, maintaining a tension that adapts to load and weather with a flexibility that rigid timber could not achieve. The Balance DC for the bridge is 12, and experienced locals cross it without looking. The bridge is used by barge-crew and silk-traders dozens of times a day, the foot-traffic creating a gentle rhythmic bounce that newcomers find unnerving.

name: Starbloom Shrine
description: The Starbloom Shrine stands at the western edge of Myruvane's forest clearing, its circular form defined by twelve sky-oak posts supporting a platform open to the sky. Air magic performed here is amplified for gust of wind (Fort DC 14), and the shrine is used for both religious ceremony and practical air-shaping work. Since the Deceivers' War, the shrine also honors Apela Kelsoe the Windrider, whose portfolio of air, travel, and luck speaks to a people twice displaced. Pasua Elf wind-dancers perform here at the Silksong Festival, their movements combining physical dance with elemental shaping in a form that is both art and offering.

name: Myruvane - Windspeaker's Lodge
description: Windspeaker Caelith Greenveil governs Myruvane from a lodge adjacent to the Aviary Gardens, its walls of living sky-oak still growing, the branches woven into structural form rather than cut. The lodge is a demonstration of the Pasua and Hasura collaboration that defines Myruvane â€” the architecture is Pasua in concept, the decorative work Hasura in execution. Greenveil receives petitioners here in the morning and spends afternoons in the Aviary overseeing silk production. Their approach to governance is as much mediation as administration, reflecting the delicate balance of two Elf peoples and a significant Halfling minority in a city defined by cooperation.

name: Myruvane - Flute Artisans' Row
description: A lane of small workshops near Melody Hollow where the makers of Myruvane's most valued export â€” woodwind instruments crafted from sky-oak â€” keep their premises. The instruments produced here are sold throughout Tidebloom Reach and exported to Kinsweave and the Twin Rivers; the best pieces find their way to performers in the most prestigious institutions on the continent. The artisans are Hasura Elves of the Foxfen refugee lineage, many of them inheriting craft traditions that were nearly lost when their ancestors' city burned. The sound of flutes being tested drifts from the workshops at all hours, and no one in Myruvane considers it a nuisance.

name: Myruvane - Barge Landing
description: The barge landing at the base of the Tideweave Bridge handles the goods flow between Myruvane's lake-shore trade and the canopy-level markets. The landing is a timber platform on the Kailian's inlet, its surface kept in good repair because the entire economy of the silk-moth trade depends on the safe unloading of food supplies and the equally safe loading of finished silk for export. Chain-guides connect the landing to the outer chain-field system, and a small chain-keeper's post here maintains the anchor ring that is Myruvane's contribution to the Mithril Chain network â€” smaller than Branmill Cove's or Velathenor's, but carefully maintained.

name: Myruvane - East Market
description: Myruvane's east-side market handles the goods that flow in and out of the city by the land routes â€” the dyed fabrics, finished instruments, and raw silk going west, and the iron fittings, preserved foods, and trade goods coming in. The market is smaller than those of the lake-shore cities but well-organized, its stalls separated by trade type and governed by a Halfling market-master whose assessments of fair value are accepted by all parties without much argument. The Halflings of Myruvane's minority community are most visible here, their commercial instincts valued in a city where most residents would rather be weaving or playing than trading.

name: Myruvane - Shrine Approach Path
description: The path leading to the Starbloom Shrine from the north is maintained as a formal processional route, its surface of smooth bark-chips replaced each spring and its border plantings of starbloom flowers (a sky-oak forest flower that opens only at night) kept meticulously. Processional lanterns on carved posts are lit for ceremonies and for the nightly meditation walk that some of Myruvane's residents practice. The path feels apart from the city even when it is clearly within it â€” the plantings create a visual and auditory buffer that makes the shrine feel more remote than it is.

name: Myruvane - Community Gathering Hall
description: The main community space of Myruvane sits between the Windspeaker's Lodge and the Starbloom Shrine, a circular platform large enough to hold several hundred people for festival gatherings. The Silksong Festival's main events take place here, with the moth-bioluminescence display viewed from the platform's edge and the musical performances staged at the center. The platform's timber is swept and maintained to a standard that reflects the pride Myruvane takes in this space â€” it is the city's face to visitors, and the Silksong Festival draws travelers from across Tidebloom Reach. Benches around the perimeter are used for informal community meetings throughout the year.

name: Myruvane - Pasua Quarter
