description: The Pasua Elf community of Myruvane occupies a section of the canopy north of Melody Hollow, their platforms distinguished by the vine-draped living structures that Pasua architecture favors. The Pasua here are primarily the descendants of those who sheltered Foxfen's Hasura refugees after the burning and subsequently joined the new settlement at Myruvane. Their relationship with the Hasura majority is close and long-established, and the cultural cross-pollination between the two communities â€” visible in music, in art, and in the shared ceremonies at the Starbloom Shrine â€” represents exactly the kind of Kin cooperation that the Deceivers' War made urgently necessary.

name: Myruvane - Lake Trail
description: A trail from the barge landing along the Kailian inlet connects Myruvane to the main lake-shore road system. The trail is overhung with Tidewoods vegetation and frequently wet from the lake's humidity, its surface maintained with cut timber laid crosswise over the muddiest sections. Herons and kingfishers are constant companions along the trail, and the inlet's still water reflects the canopy in clear conditions. The trail is used for the fish-carrier traffic and for the occasional visitor who arrives by water rather than by road.

name: Myruvane - North Gate and Road
description: The north gate of Myruvane opens onto the deeper Tidewoods, the road beyond following the sky-oak canopy's edge toward Tiravel and the northern approaches. The gate is staffed by Pasua Elf wardens whose Kin-sense monitoring is particularly acute â€” a reflection of Myruvane's awareness that it sits between the living cities and the haunted northeast. Outbound travelers are advised of current patrol reports; inbound travelers are assessed with the thoroughness that a city built by refugees applies to its borders. The road north is the fastest overland route between the southern settlements and Tiravel.

name: Myruvane - Foxfen Memorial Grove
description: Behind the Starbloom Shrine, shielded from the main paths by a screen of living trees that Pasua Elf gardeners have shaped over thirty years, the Foxfen Memorial Grove is Myruvane's most private public space. Awakened tree-stumps from Foxfen's burned grove have been symbolically replanted here as saplings, their growth encouraged by the same nature-shaping that saved a handful of Foxfen's people before the flames consumed Druid Sylvara Greenbough. Greenbough's name is carved at the grove's entrance on a simple stone marker, and the vine-wrapped stone in the grove's center is said to be a fragment of the vine barrier she wove in her final moments.

name: Myruvane - Artisan Market
description: A market for the finished artistic goods that Myruvane's craftspeople produce: instruments, dyed fabrics, silk garments, and the small decorative moth-wing pieces â€” preserved in resin â€” that are a Myruvane specialty and a popular trade item. The artisan market is smaller and higher-priced than the east market, its goods positioned for buyers with specific taste and adequate funds. The market is staffed by the makers themselves rather than by agents, which makes the transactions more personal and the price negotiation more interesting. A commissioned instrument from a Myruvane maker takes six to twelve weeks and costs accordingly.

name: Myruvane - Silk Processing Hall
description: The silk processing hall converts raw silk-moth output into finished thread and then into fabric, a sequence of operations requiring the precise management of humidity, temperature, and the air-elemental currents that are Myruvane's industrial advantage. Workers here are specialists trained over years, and the processing sequence they follow was developed collaboratively by Hasura and Pasua weavers after the Silksong Festival of the first post-war generation. The hall runs two shifts and produces enough finished silk to meet domestic demand and a significant export quota. Waste silk fibers are composted into the Aviary Gardens' soil.

name: Myruvane - Halfling Community House
description: The ten percent Halfling minority of Myruvane maintains a community house at the forest floor level, a space that serves as hostel for visiting Halflings, gathering place for the local community, and informal embassy for Halfling interests in the Windspeaker's governance. The house is furnished in a combination of Halfling comfort-traditions and the practical constraints of sky-oak forest living, and the result is pleasant rather than compromised. The community's connection to Semyon the Hearth Mother is maintained here through a small shrine in the kitchen that is the house's most consistently warm room.

name: Myruvane - Herb-Dye Garden
description: A hillside garden terraced into the forest margin south of the Aviary produces the plant-based dyes that give Myruvane's fabrics their distinctive palette. The terraces are arranged by color family â€” blue and green plants above, red and yellow below â€” and the garden is arranged so that water flows downhill through channels carved into the terrace walls, watering the lower sections with the nutrient-rich runoff from above. The garden is maintained by a joint Hasura-Pasua team whose botanical knowledge is impressive in its depth and whose willingness to experiment has produced several new dye colors in the past decade.

name: Myruvane - Storage Lofts
description: The storage lofts of Myruvane are elevated platforms in the canopy where dried and packaged goods are kept above the forest floor humidity. The lofts store finished silk awaiting export, dried instruments wrapped in preserving cloth, and the food and raw material reserves that give the city its buffer against trade disruptions. The loft system is managed by a Halfling logistics coordinator whose spatial memory and organizational instinct are considered city assets by the Windspeaker. Access to the secure lofts requires authorization; the common storage areas are accessible to residents during daylight.

name: Myruvane - Lakeside Dock
description: Myruvane's main dock on the Kailian shore handles the seasonal exchange of goods that supplements the barge-landing at the Tideweave Bridge. At Tide Turn, when traffic is highest, additional barge berths are staked out from the dock's ends. The dock is timber, built on a stone foundation, and the Pekakarlik maintenance contract is renewed annually. A signal-post at the dock's end communicates with the watch at Velathenor and Darnavar by flag, and the dock-master coordinates chain-field access with the harbor masters of both neighboring cities to prevent scheduling conflicts during the critical planting and harvest periods.

name: Myruvane - Bard's Corner
description: A platform in the canopy's mid-level, Bard's Corner has no official status but considerable cultural weight. It is where Myruvane's musicians gather informally after the day's work, where instruments are tested and compared, where songs in progress are workshopped, and where the oral tradition that records Tidebloom's history is maintained and extended. The atmospheric quality of air-elemental amplification near the Aviary makes this a particularly good performance space, and the informal concerts that happen here on warm evenings draw audiences from across the city. Harreem's influence is felt here too â€” not all the songs are cheerful.

name: Myruvane - South Road
description: The south road from Myruvane descends through the thinning Tidewoods toward the Kailian shore and the trade connections to Darnavar and the wider region. The road is maintained by both Myruvane and Darnavar, their shared interest in commerce overcoming any political differences. Milestones mark the route, and a supply cache maintained by both cities is stocked at the midpoint. The road is safe by day and patrolled; by night it is recommended to camp rather than travel, as the Tidewoods' nocturnal character changes in ways that experienced travelers respect.

name: Myruvane - Windspeaker's Overlook
description: A platform at the top of Myruvane's tallest sky-oak, accessible only by the senior canopy staff and by Windspeaker Greenveil's invitation, the Overlook provides the broadest view available in the city. On clear days both Velathenor to the north and Darnavar to the south-east are visible as breaks in the forest canopy where the Kailian shore reflects light. The Overlook is used for the Windspeaker's air-shaping meditations and for the weather assessments that inform Myruvane's agricultural planning. Greenveil finds the isolation necessary â€” the governance of a city built by two generations of refugees requires spaces where the weight of that history can be held without audience.

name: Myruvane - Gate Road Junction
description: The junction at Myruvane's eastern boundary where the forest trails meet the lake-shore road and the road to the north gate converge into a single point. A waypost here is more heavily carved than most â€” both Hasura and Pasua directional notation are used, making it readable to both canopy and forest communities. The junction is also where travelers from Darnavar arrive and where outbound caravans form up before departure. A small fire-ring at the junction is maintained for travelers caught out after dark, and the local community checks it weekly to ensure the fire-making supplies are stocked.

name: Floatmarket
description: The Floatmarket is Darnavar's most distinctive institution and the most unusual market in Tidebloom Reach: a network of rafted stalls tethered to the Mithril Chain anchor-posts on the Kailian's surface, rising and falling with the lake's seasonal cycle. The stalls sell nets, lake pearls, Riverstone Amulets, fish, and the dried-reed goods that the Eruskan Human majority of Darnavar has specialized in since the Concordant era. The market is busiest in the morning when the fishing boats return, and the sound of trade calls, chain-metal striking wood, and the ever-present lapping water creates a din that residents find comfortable and visitors find overwhelming.

name: Reedtemple
description: The Reedtemple is built from the bundled and shaped reed-grass of the Kailian's margin, its walls and roof a dense weave that is surprisingly weather-resistant and beautifully textured. The temple amplifies water magic â€” create water cast here is noticeably more powerful, the flow fuller and the purity greater. The temple has expanded significantly since the Deceivers' War, now including a full sanctuary to Kaile'a, Mistress of Waves, alongside the older Lady of the Sea dedication. In Darnavar, where water, trade, and community define daily life, Kaile'a's combination of wave-mastery and practical divine assistance is the most personally resonant theology the city has encountered.

name: Boatwright Quay
description: The eastern end of Darnavar's waterfront is occupied by the Boatwright Quay, where the Pekakarlik Dwarf boatwrights who are fifteen percent of the city's population build and repair the riverboats and fishing craft that the Kailian community depends on. The Quay smells of fresh timber, caulking compound, and the particular resinous tang of the Tidewoods timber used for boat construction. River Spears â€” weapons that grant a +2 attack bonus in water â€” are made here and sold from a workshop at the Quay's landward end; Tarvek Wen's oath-mark is carved above the main slipway, honoring the god of justice and craftsmanship whose principles the Pekakarlik hold as professional standards.

name: Darnavar - South Shore
description: The southern shore of Darnavar's Kailian frontage is where the city's tidal fields begin. At low water, the dark silt of the exposed seabed is visible here, and the chain-guides from the Mithril Chain anchor-posts lead directly to the chain-driven planting platforms that operate these fields. Mayor Teven Saltmark has made the southern fields a priority for the post-war recovery â€” the drowned ruins of Mossgrove lie below the water's surface not far to the south, and the mayor's plan to use Mira Seadancer's staff (if it can be recovered) to strengthen these water defenses is not publicly stated but widely understood.

name: Lakemist Spire - Base
description: The Lakemist Spire takes its name from the morning mist that forms over the Kailian at this latitude and rolls inland over the low-shore buildings in the cold months, wrapping the spire's base in grey. The tower is built on a stone foundation that extends into the lake itself, its base reinforced against the high-water season storms by iron buttressing added after the Deceivers' War. The tower's watch role is doubled: it monitors both lake approaches and the submerged southern margin where the drowned ruins of Mossgrove lie below the surface, the occasional unnatural shimmer from those depths a subject of watchman reports that the Mayor reviews personally.

name: Darnavar - Shrine Quarter
description: The block between the Reedtemple and the Boatwright Quay holds the cluster of smaller shrines that serve Darnavar's religious diversity. Shrines to the Lady of the Sea, Kaile'a, and Tarvek Wen anchor the quarter, alongside smaller alcoves for Semyon the Hearth Mother and the Halfling community's private devotions. The most recent addition is a carved marker for Mira Seadancer, the sorcerer who died guiding Mossgrove's last barges to safety; it is not a formal temple, but the candles left at its base and the small waterproof tokens placed against the carving make its status clear. The shrine quarter is the quietest part of Darnavar at any hour.

name: Darnavar - Boatwright Workshop
description: The main boat-construction workshop of the Boatwright Quay is a long, high-ceilinged building where hulls in various stages of completion sit on construction frames. The Pekakarlik master boatwright presides over a team of four journeymen and two apprentices, the work organized so that multiple boats are always in progress at once â€” the region's demand for replacement craft never fully catches up with the supply. The designs used here have been refined over five generations, and the current plans represent the accumulated experience of every Kailian storm, every tidal challenge, and every repair job that has taught the boatwrights what the lake requires.

name: Darnavar - Chain-Field Landing
description: The landing platform at Darnavar's southern chain-field access point handles the coordination between the city's barge operations and the main Mithril Chain network. A chain-keeper's post here reports directly to Mayor Saltmark's office on the condition of the anchor-rings and the status of the planting-season barge schedule. The post maintains a log of chain-anchor maintenance records that complements the Branmill Cove and Velathenor records, creating a comprehensive picture of the whole system's health. The most recent entries note three anchor-points showing unusual wear â€” an item flagged for priority inspection before the next Tide Turn.

name: Lakemist Spire - Watchtower
description: From the top of the Lakemist Spire, the southern Kailian is visible on clear days all the way to the submerged outline of Mossgrove's old waterfront â€” a dark smudge below the lake's surface that has never fully faded despite a generation of silt accumulation. The watchman on duty here is trained to report any unusual light from those depths, any movement of the unnatural kind that the post's standing orders describe in terms careful enough to avoid the word 'haunted' while meaning exactly that. The view north shows Darnavar's full extent and the Floatmarket's rafts on the lake surface, ordinary and reassuring.

name: Darnavar - Mayor's Hall
description: Mayor Teven Saltmark â€” Eruskan Human, Sorcerer 6, the man who united Darnavar's post-war refugees into a functioning community â€” governs from a hall that is more comfortable than ceremonial. The hall's central room serves as both reception space and council chamber, its walls hung with charts of the Kailian's southern reaches and a large map showing both the living city and the drowned outline of Mossgrove below. Saltmark's desk holds a copy of the traveling bard's report about Mira Seadancer's staff and three different draft plans for recovering it, each with a different risk assessment. The Mayor is direct, pragmatic, and acutely aware that Darnavar's post-war resilience is not guaranteed.

name: Darnavar - Pekakarlik Quarter
description: The Pekakarlik community of Darnavar is centered near the Boatwright Quay, their neighborhood's low-ceilinged houses and precision-organized workshops clustering together in the practical way of Dwarven settlements everywhere. The Pekakarlik here are primarily boatwrights and chain-maintenance workers, their skills essential to the city's lake-dependent economy. The quarter has a shrine-forge to Gonmareck Ritler at its center â€” a combination smithy and worship space that is distinctly Pekakarlik in its theology, since the god of craftsmanship is understood here as best honored through excellent work. The quality of tools produced in this quarter is regional standard.

name: Darnavar - Net-Maker's Yard
description: An open-air yard on the Kailian's edge where Darnavar's net production takes place. Woven nets â€” fishing nets, the finer mesh used for the chain-field planting platforms, and the heavy cargo nets used by the Floatmarket's loading cranes â€” are produced here by a workforce that is mostly Eruskan Human women, a tradition going back to the Concordant era when net-making was one of the few skilled trades accessible to all Kin equally. The yard has a distinctive rhythmic sound â€” the wooden shuttle-blocks and the pull-and-tie of knotwork â€” that carries over the water on calm days and is part of the aural texture of Darnavar.

name: Darnavar - Market Street
description: The main commercial street of Darnavar runs from the Mayor's Hall to the Floatmarket landing, lined with the permanent shops and trading offices that serve the city's daily needs. Provisions, rope, lamp oil, and sailing hardware share the street with cloth merchants, food-sellers, and the Halfling traders who stock items brought in from Velathenor and Branmill Cove. The street is paved with Pekakarlik-laid lake-stone and wide enough for loaded carts, and the shop-signs are mostly painted timber rather than carved stone â€” functional, easy to replace, and repainted frequently enough that the street stays bright despite the constant lake humidity.

name: Darnavar - Community Hall
description: Darnavar's community hall was built in the first years after the Deceivers' War specifically to house the refugee community that had more than doubled the city's population. It is larger than a city of 4,200 would normally require and reflects the priorities of a community that had just watched disunity cost four cities everything. The hall is used for civic assemblies, for the Reedwave Festival's indoor events, and for the regular dinners that Mayor Saltmark hosts for the different community groups â€” a practice that started as political necessity and became genuine social tradition. The hall is fully accessible at street level, its design specifically chosen to be welcoming to all body types.

name: Darnavar - Eruskan Quarter
description: The Eruskan Human majority of Darnavar occupies the city's inland quarter, their houses and workshops organized along practical straight lanes. The Eruskan families here include both the original Darnavar population and the Whistlebank and Mossgrove refugees who swelled the city after the burnings, and three decades have blended the communities to the point where most residents know both lineages. The cultural markers that distinguish Mossgrove from Whistlebank from original Darnavar are mainly visible in festival customs and old-family cooking traditions rather than in any social division. The city that grief built has worked at unity with a determination that less-haunted places rarely match.

name: Darnavar - Halfling Quarter
description: The Halfling community of Darnavar â€” ten percent of the population â€” maintains its quarter near the Floatmarket, their connections to the lake trade giving them economic centrality that their numbers alone would not. The quarter is the city's most cheerfully decorated, a community tradition that serves partly as aesthetic preference and partly as a deliberate counterweight to the grief that marks Darnavar's collective memory. The Reedwave Festival's preparations always begin in this quarter, and the flower-weaving, lantern-making, and food preparation that precede the festival are community events that draw participation from all of Darnavar's groups.

name: Darnavar - East Gate
description: The east gate of Darnavar opens onto the road that follows the Kailian shore east toward the Sundrift Veil and the trade routes connecting Tidebloom Reach to Kinsweave and the wider west. The gate is the most active of Darnavar's exits, handling the caravan traffic that is the city's commercial lifeline. A customs post here assesses incoming goods and records departures, and the duty officer maintains the watch log that feeds into the Mayor's security briefings. The view east shows the lake shore opening up as the Tidewoods thin, and on clear days the Sundrift Veil's lighter canopy is visible as a pale smudge on the horizon.

name: Darnavar - Healer's House
description: Darnavar's healing facility is well-resourced relative to the city's size, a legacy of the post-war period when the influx of traumatized refugees created medical needs beyond what conventional civic provision could handle. The current staff of three healers â€” one Eruskan Human cleric of Tarvek Wen, one Pekakarlik physic, and a young Halfling apothecary's apprentice â€” can manage most medical emergencies and provide the therapeutic conversations that trauma survivors sometimes need alongside physical treatment. The healer's house has a small library of medical texts and a garden of medicinal plants maintained by the apothecary's apprentice.

name: Darnavar - Armorer and Supply
description: A combined armor and equipment shop near the Halfling Quarter serves both the city's resident population and travelers passing through on the lake-shore trade route. The armorer carries practical gear â€” leather, ring-mail, river-knives â€” and the supply section has a strong inventory of sailing and water-travel equipment. River Spears from the Boatwright Quay are available for purchase here, and the shopkeeper maintains a commission arrangement with the Pekakarlik boatwrights for custom weapon orders. The shop stays open later than most Darnavar businesses, serving the evening traffic from the Floatmarket.

name: Darnavar - Floatmarket Landing
description: The stone ramp at the Floatmarket's landward edge allows goods to flow between the lake surface and the city's inland market street. Carts are loaded here from the crane that lifts cargo from the rafted stalls below, and the ramp is always occupied during trading hours with the traffic of the city's commercial metabolism. A small office at the ramp's top handles the Floatmarket's stall assignments and the security arrangements that keep the lake-surface market viable. Mayor Saltmark's office receives a daily report from this post on unusual activity near the Floatmarket, since the stalls' proximity to the Mossgrove approach makes them a potential entry point for anything that might rise from the drowned ruins.

name: Darnavar - Reed Harvesting Dock
description: A dedicated dock on the city's western margin handles the seasonal harvest of the reed-grass that is used in net-making, building construction, and the Reedtemple's maintenance. Teams go out in flat-bottomed boats at the appropriate season and harvest the reeds that grow in the Kailian's marginal shallows, returning to this dock for processing and distribution. The dock has a drying yard attached where harvested reeds are spread and turned until they reach the flexibility and strength required for working. The smell of drying reed is pleasant and distinctive, and the dock is an informal gathering place for workers during the harvest season.

name: Darnavar - Tidal Fields Observation Point
description: A raised earthwork on Darnavar's southern edge gives citizens and chain-field workers a clear view of the planting fields and the barge operations below. The observation point is used for both agricultural monitoring and as the vantage for the Reedwave Festival's Tide Turn ceremony, at which Mayor Saltmark leads the city's thanks for the harvest. From here, the submerged outline of Mossgrove is visible on calm days as a dark pattern in the lake's southern margin â€” the city that drowned when Lord Rakahda hesitated and Mira Seadancer paid the price. Darnavar does not look away from that outline. They keep it visible on purpose.

name: Darnavar - Caravaner's Inn
description: The Caravaner's Inn at Darnavar's market street junction serves the merchant traffic that moves along the Kailian shore road, its rooms designed for the practical needs of people who spend most of their lives traveling. Secure storage, a stable-yard, and a common room where route information is freely exchanged make this the most commercially useful accommodation on the southern Kailian circuit. The innkeeper is a former Kinsweave caravan-master who settled in Darnavar after the war and knows every road condition and bandit report on the routes between here and the western cities. Her common room noticeboards are more current than most official sources.

name: Darnavar - North Road to Myruvane
description: The road north from Darnavar passes through the Tidewoods' southern fringe and eventually connects to Myruvane, the route used by silk-traders and instrument-buyers as well as the chain-maintenance teams who service the network between the two cities. The road is maintained jointly and is in good repair, its surface of packed gravel adequate for loaded carts in most weather. The Tidewoods close in quickly north of the city, and the comfortable openness of Darnavar's lake-shore position gives way to the filtered green light and layered sounds of the forest within a quarter-mile of the gate.

name: Darnavar - Mossgrove Approach Shore
description: The southernmost accessible point of Darnavar's shoreline, this stretch of the Kailian margin is where the lake deepens toward the drowned ruins of Mossgrove. The water here is always slightly cooler than the northern shore, and the reeds are sparser and less healthy, as if the lake itself is affected by what lies below. On moonless nights, some residents report seeing a dim glow from the depths â€” the reawakening of Mira Seadancer's staff, if the traveling bard's report is correct. The shore is not restricted, but it is not comfortable, and the people of Darnavar who walk here do so with a particular quiet purposefulness, as if they are paying a visit rather than taking a walk.

name: Hillmeet - Charred Gateway
description: What was once a sky-oak arch marking the entrance to Hillmeet has burned to a pair of blackened stumps, their surfaces fused by the DÃ³mnathar sorcery that turned the Wind Lord's own air into fire. The char goes deep â€” not surface burn, but structural destruction that no natural fire produces. Ash has settled into every crevice and the ground between the stumps is grey rather than dark, as if even the soil has been changed. A faint smell of burning lingers here even after decades, and the wind that passes through the stumps makes a sound somewhere between a whisper and a moan.

name: Hillmeet - Fallen Canopy Platform
description: The great canopy platform that once served as Hillmeet's main gathering space has fallen, its sky-oak supports burned through at the base, the platform itself lying at an angle against two of the surviving stumps. The wood is black and hard, fire-preserved in an ironic way â€” the burning that destroyed it has made the surviving timber impervious to further decay. Sand Wraiths are drawn to this platform, the largest of the burned structures; their forms flicker at the edges of vision, grief made tangible, reaching toward anything that carries elemental warmth. The air here is perceptibly colder than the surrounding forest.

name: Hillmeet - Bard's Platform Ruins
description: The platform where Bard Aerith Windweave made her last stand is identifiable by the unusual burn pattern â€” the wood here burned not outward from below but inward from above, as if the fire came down from the sky. Her flute's final note is said to have echoed here for three days after the burning, heard by refugees miles away. The platform's remains hold a faint harmonic resonance that air magic perceives as a sustained low tone. Searchers who have come looking for the flute itself have found nothing, though disturbed debris suggests others have searched before them. The resonance intensifies near the platform's center.

name: Hillmeet - Storm Wisp Warren
description: The collapsed lower level of the Hillmeet archive hall has become a nesting site for Storm Wisps, the CR 2 electrical spirits that are a byproduct of the unnatural burning. Their blue-white flickers are visible in the debris at all hours, and those who approach too closely find the air crackling with static charge. The Wisps are not malicious in the way the Sand Wraiths are â€” they appear to be more confused than angry, their behavior suggesting something trapped rather than something hunting. Lore-Keeper Windbloom's notes suggest the Wisps may be bound to a specific artifact still buried in the archive debris.

name: Hillmeet - Lord Skysong's Spire Base
description: The base of Lord Pryce Skysong's personal tower â€” the seat from which he declared the war 'not a battle we needs to see' â€” is the most intact structure in Hillmeet, an accidental monument to the arrogance that cost the city everything. The stone base survived the burning that took the sky-oak upper levels, and the rooms within are scorched but accessible. Sand Wraiths are more concentrated here than anywhere else in the ruins, as if the restless dead hold the location of their lord's failure in particular contempt. The walls of the base bear his fire-damaged banner, its device no longer readable.

name: Hillmeet - Flooded Cellar Archive
description: Beneath the collapsed archive, water from decades of rain has pooled in the cellar, creating a shallow indoor lake among the fallen shelves and burned scroll-cases. Some items have survived in the water â€” wind-silk is nearly indestructible in immersion â€” but retrieving them requires wading through the dark, cold pool while Sand Wraiths circle overhead. A search of the cellar (Search DC 20) reveals a sealed scroll-case with its contents intact: partial wind-silk scrolls from Hillmeet's song archive, the kind that Lore-Keeper Windbloom has spent a decade reconstructing from fragments.

name: Hillmeet - Tomb of Kings Approach
description: The northeastern edge of Hillmeet's ruins gives way to the deeper forest that leads to the Tomb of Kings, the ancient Giant burial site that predates the Kin by uncounted centuries. The transition is marked by a change in the forest's character â€” the Tidewoods undergrowth thins and the trees grow larger and older, their bark patterned with the pale lichen that seems to grow only near sites of significant elemental density. The Kin-sense anomalies reported by Tiravel's Canopy Watch are most pronounced in this area, the dead-zone patches forming a loose corridor toward the Tomb that no patrol has yet been willing to follow to its terminus.

name: Hillmeet - Tomb of Kings - Outer Precinct
description: The outer boundary of the Tomb of Kings is marked by standing stones twice the height of a tall human, each one inscribed with Giant rune-work so deeply carved that the characters cast shadows even in overcast light. The stones are spaced to form a rough circle, and the ground within is clear of undergrowth â€” not burned clear, but simply absent, as if the turf has chosen not to grow here. The Hasura of Tiravel consider this sacred ground and the stonework here shows evidence of recent careful tending. Guardian constructs â€” described in patrol reports as 'stone forms that move' â€” have been seen near the outer standing stones at night.

name: Foxfen - Vine-Strangled Gate
description: What was once Foxfen's grove entrance â€” a living arch of awakened trees that greeted visitors with the warmth of genuine consciousness â€” is now a tangle of dead and re-growing vegetation, the original awakened trees killed by the alien fire that defied the Lady of Fire's natural harmony. The dead trunks are thick with parasitic growth, their wood supporting a new, unconscious ecosystem that has slowly reclaimed the physical space if not the spiritual character. The air smells of rot and new growth in uneasy combination, and Storm Wisps drift between the dead trunks in the grey light.

name: Foxfen - Druid's Last Stand
description: The clearing where Druid Sylvara Greenbough wove her vine barrier is marked by the remains of that barrier â€” long dead now, but preserved in the same strange way that some things in the Ashen Hollows are preserved, as if the elemental power of the act has maintained a physical echo. The vines are bleached white and hard as bone, their curves still tracing the arc of the barrier that saved a handful of Foxfen's people. A Sand Wraith of unusual intensity haunts this clearing, its behavior suggesting something more purposeful than the grief-driven wandering of the other Wraiths in the ruins. It does not attack immediately, but it watches.

name: Foxfen - Ogre-Burned Grove
description: The section of Foxfen that was set ablaze by ogres and evil humans shows a different character from the DÃ³mnathar sorcery burns â€” this fire behaved like fire should, consuming and leaving ash, rather than the structural destruction of alien magic. What remains is a wide clearing of black soil, regrowing slowly with the pioneer plants that colonize burned ground first: fireweed, ash-grass, and the small yellow flowers that the Hasura call 'Foxfen blooms' in wry memorial. The clearing is oddly peaceful compared to the rest of the ruins, as if the natural fire has settled whatever spirit it disturbed.

name: Foxfen - Deceiver Orb Chamber
description: The center of Foxfen's collapsed main grove-hall, accessible through a collapse-gap in the dead-fall timber, holds the remains of the alien magic that the Deceivers used here. A shadow-etched orb â€” Lord Verith's bane, a Deceiver artifact â€” is described in Tiravel's patrol reports as still present, still emitting a faint dark shimmer that disrupts Kin-sense in a ten-foot radius. Storm Wisps cluster around it, apparently attracted to or imprisoned by its residual power. Retrieving the orb (Search DC 20, Disable Device DC 18 to contain the Kin-sense disruption) would require disabling or dispersing the Wisps first, and the effect on the Foxfen ruins if the orb is removed is unknown.

name: Foxfen - Regrown Margin
description: The eastern edge of Foxfen's ruins is the furthest from the original burning and shows the most natural recovery, the Tidewoods' normal vegetation slowly reasserting itself among the dead stumps. Trees are growing back â€” not sky-oaks yet, but the pioneer species that prepare soil for sky-oak establishment over decades. The margin is not safe â€” Sand Wraiths range this far from the ruins' center â€” but it is the least oppressive part of Foxfen, and the birdsong here is almost normal. Almost. The birds fall silent at irregular intervals in a pattern that corresponds, for those tracking it, with the Sand Wraith patrol patterns.

name: Foxfen - Lord Verith's Hall Ruins
description: The ruins of Lord Verith's hall â€” from which he declared he would defend only his own walls â€” are the most substantial surviving structure in Foxfen. Stone foundation walls still stand to head height, the sky-oak upper stories long since burned. The interior is exposed to the sky, filled with dead-fall and decades of leaf-accumulation. A Sand Wraith of strong intensity haunts the hall specifically, its behavior suggesting it may be associated with a resident who died here defending the walls that Verith claimed he would protect and then failed to defend. Personal items partially visible in the debris include a melted silver chain that might be recoverable.

name: Foxfen - Eastern Trail
description: The trail that once connected Foxfen to the wider forest road system is overgrown but passable, its path still visible as a slightly depressed line through the regrown vegetation. The trail is used occasionally by Tiravel's long-range patrols, who regard reaching it as the outer limit of responsible patrol range given the current Kin-sense anomaly situation. Kin-sense is unreliable along this trail â€” not dead, but flickering, as if something is moving at the edge of perception. The trail connects north to the deeper forest and east toward the Stonewind Plains' edge.

name: Foxfen - Northern Ruins
description: The northernmost structures of Foxfen are the least documented and the least visited of the Ashen Hollows ruins. Whatever stood here â€” residential platforms, storage structures, or community spaces â€” has been reduced to undifferentiated debris by burning and decades of weather. The undergrowth has advanced furthest here, the ruins more absorbed into the forest matrix than elsewhere. Sand Wraiths appear less frequently in this section, which patrol reports attribute either to less intense grief-anchor here or to the Kin-sense disruption being strong enough to confuse even the Wraiths' orientation.

name: Whistlebank - Silted Harbor
description: The harbor of Whistlebank was choked by DÃ³mnathar magic that turned the Kailian's own silt against the city, filling the channel until the barges were stranded and the city's evacuation route was compromised. Decades later, the harbor is still silted â€” a broad, shallow delta of accumulated sediment where the water barely covers the old dock foundations. The Mithril Chain that served this harbor is still here, its end anchor buried under fifteen feet of silt, one of the missing chain-points visible on old Branmill Cove charts. Sand Wraiths wade through the silted shallows at night, their forms incomplete where they intersect with the accumulated sediment.

name: Whistlebank - Stranded Barge Row
description: Several chain-barges from Whistlebank's fleet were stranded by the silting and left where they sat when the evacuation completed and no one came back. They are still here, their flat hulls settled deep into the silt, their chain-guide rings rusted to the Mithril Chain links they were attached to when the harbor clogged. The barges have become platforms for the sand-grass that grows in tidal shallows, their surfaces covered in vegetation that gives them a ghostly organic appearance. Marin Wavecrest covered the evacuation from these decks before her fog-conjuring drew the DÃ³mnathar's attention and the killing spell that sank her ship.

name: Whistlebank - Market Quarter Ruins
description: Whistlebank was a thriving Eruskan port, and its market quarter's ruins reflect that prosperity in the scale of what was destroyed. Stone building foundations line the lane at regular intervals, the buildings they supported burned but the foundations intact, creating a grid of knee-high stone walls that maps the city's commercial layout exactly. A searching party (Search DC 18) might find trade-goods sealed in the old storage cellars â€” salted fish, preserved grain, trade-cloth â€” still protected by the same stone construction that saved the foundations. The Sand Wraiths here move with a purposeful desperation that makes them more dangerous than their counterparts in other ruins.

name: Whistlebank - Captain's Quay
description: The quay where Marin Wavecrest staged her fog-magic defense has a different feel from the rest of Whistlebank's ruins â€” not less dangerous, but charged with a different emotional quality. The sand wraiths here are calmer, less frantic, as if the memory of someone who acted with clarity and purpose has a stabilizing effect even on grief-formed undead. A Riverstone Amulet with an unusual sea-green luster is embedded in the quay's weathered stone near the bollard where Wavecrest is recorded as having taken her last stand. Removing it (Search DC 20) would require close contact with that space and whatever it remembers.

name: Whistlebank - Residential Ruins
description: The residential quarter of Whistlebank was built close to the market for the convenience of the fishing families who worked both. The houses were timber-framed over stone foundations, and the timbers burned completely, leaving only the foundations in a dense pattern that makes navigation difficult. The fog that Wavecrest conjured to cover the evacuation never fully lifted from this part of the ruins â€” or rather, something that looks like fog persists here, cold and unnatural, reducing visibility to thirty feet even in clear weather. The fog does not obstruct Kin-sense, but it muffles sound and makes distance-judgment difficult.

name: Whistlebank - Harbor Master's Ruins
description: The Harbor Master's office, where Lord Alprenter received the reports of the Deceiver advance and dismissed them as 'unlikely,' is one of the more substantial ruins in Whistlebank. The stone walls stand to window-height, and the interior still holds the remains of the Harbor Master's equipment: a rusted tide-gauge, the corroded remnants of a mirror-signal system, and a board where the last shipping manifest was posted and never taken down. The manifest is still partially legible on the weather-preserved wood: vessels cleared for departure, the morning of the city's last day.

name: Whistlebank - Eastern Ruins
description: The eastern margin of Whistlebank's ruins is where the DÃ³mnathar magic hit first, and the character of the destruction here is different from the harbor section â€” more intense, less explicable by conventional understanding of fire and flood. The stone is discolored in patterns that no natural process produces, and the silt that accumulated in the harbor extends inland here, as if the silting was not merely hydraulic but intentional, the earth and water conspiring under direction. Sand Wraiths in this section show agitation rather than grief, their movements suggesting fresh disturbance rather than settled haunting.

name: Mossgrove - Drowned Approach
description: The road to Mossgrove disappears under water before the old city boundary is reached â€” the unnatural tides summoned by DÃ³mnathar sorcerers that overwhelmed the lake-town never fully receded, leaving the outer reaches permanently submerged. The water here is shallow enough to wade (Swim DC 10), but the footing is uncertain and the visibility below the surface poor. Drowned vegetation from the original inundation still stands in the shallower sections, its dead branches reaching upward through the water like a forest of intentions. Sand Wraiths move here in their underwater form, their touch desiccating even in immersion.

name: Mossgrove - Submerged Market Square
description: Three feet of water covers what was once Mossgrove's main market square, the lake-stone paving visible through the murky water when the light is right. Stall foundations stand as underwater obstacles at irregular intervals, and the goods that did not float away â€” stone weights, iron tools, the heavier trade goods â€” lie where they fell when the water rose. A glow visible on moonless nights from the southern part of the square is consistent with reports of Mira Seadancer's staff reawakening; the staff's last known position was near the market square's well, which still stands to chest height above the waterline.

name: Mossgrove - Partially Submerged Docks
description: The eastern dock of Mossgrove is partially above water, its pilings standing but its surface rotten and unreliable underfoot. The dock extends over deeper water than the rest of the submerged ruins, and in the depths below the outer dock sections, the silhouettes of Mossgrove's sunken barges are visible in clear water. Sorcerer Mira Seadancer's last known route took her along this dock, guiding the last barges out as the unnatural tide rose around her. A Deceiver relic (Search DC 20) is visible from the dock's end, lodged in the sunken barge nearest the dock â€” whatever it was that reacted to the staff's reawakening.

name: Mossgrove - Lord Rakahda's Hall - Flooded
description: The hall where Lord Rakahda questioned why the races were being pushed 'beyond the brink' and then did nothing is now waist-deep in the unnatural water that his inaction allowed to rise. The flood-level inside the hall is higher than outside, a physical impossibility that continues to baffle those who have investigated â€” the water inside is maintained at a higher level by whatever DÃ³mnathar magic was anchored here, as if the hall is being deliberately kept in judgment. Sand Wraiths are densest around this hall, and they behave with an urgency in this location that suggests the memory of a specific failure rather than generalized grief.

name: Mossgrove - Eastern Shore - Submerged
description: The eastern shore of Mossgrove is among the deepest drowned sections, requiring actual swimming (Swim DC 15) to navigate. The bottom here is covered in the mosswork that gave the city its name, still growing under the unnatural conditions of the permanent inundation â€” a dark, thick carpet of aquatic moss that covers the original paving entirely. Shapes visible in the moss include the outlines of items from the flooding: furniture, equipment, personal possessions of the residents who evacuated without time to gather everything. A careful search (Search DC 18) might recover any of dozens of items, and most of them are in better condition than they have any right to be.

name: Mossgrove - The Staff's Location
description: The well at Mossgrove's market square stands at the waterline, its stone rim barely above the lake's surface in the current season. Below the water, clearly visible to anyone who swims down to look (Swim DC 12), Mira Seadancer's staff rests wedged between the well's interior stones, its lower end touching the aquifer. The amber glow that residents of Darnavar occasionally see from the southern shore emanates from here. The staff's magical resonance is strong enough to feel from the surface with active Kin-sense. A Deceiver relic (Search DC 20 from below) has apparently settled near the staff, the two objects reacting to each other across a few feet of drowned stone.

name: Tidewoods - North Road from Branmill
description: The road north from Branmill Cove enters the Whispering Tidewoods within a quarter-mile of the city gate, the transition from open lakeside to dense forest canopy happening quickly. The road is well-maintained in its first mile â€” civic maintenance responsibility extends this far â€” and the way-markers are freshly painted. The humidity of the Tidewoods is immediate and distinctive, the air thickening and the sounds of the lake fading behind the forest's layered voices. Patrol boot-prints in the soft shoulders of the road show regular use by Branmill Cove's watchmen.

name: Tidewoods - Northern Trail Junction
description: A forest junction where the road from Branmill Cove splits â€” northwest toward the Stonewind Plains and the approaches to the Tomb of Kings, northeast toward the Ashen Hollows. A carved post at the junction marks both directions, the northeastern arm bearing an additional warning: 'Ashen Hollows â€” Sand Wraith Territory â€” Proceed with Caution.' The western branch is cleaner and more traveled, the northeastern one showing signs of hesitation in boot-tracks that approach and stop, approach and stop. The forest here is at its most typical: sky-oaks filtering jade-green light, orchid-laden undergrowth, the distant call of a lake-heron.

name: Tidewoods - Western Trail to Stonewind Plains
description: The western trail from the forest junction follows the transition zone between the Tidewoods and the Stonewind Plains, the canopy thinning and the undergrowth changing from orchid-and-moss to the harder, drier vegetation of savannah. The transition takes several miles, and this stretch is in the middle of it â€” tall trees still overhead but sparser, and the wind off the plains detectable as a different quality of air underneath the forest's humid breath. Grazing animals from the plains sometimes venture into this margin for the forest's shade and water, and their trails crisscross the road.

name: Tidewoods - Ashen Hollows Approach Road
description: The road northeast toward the Ashen Hollows narrows as it goes, the forest closing in as the maintained portion gives way to the unmanaged growth. The smell of the Tidewoods is still present but mixed with something else â€” the faint ghost of ash that the Ashen Hollows never fully lose, carried south on the air even in good weather. Kin-sense is reliable here but the experienced traveler keeps it active: the first dead-zone patches begin within a mile of this point, the earliest warning that the haunted northeast is near. The light is slightly different in this direction, greyer even when the sun is high.

name: Stonewind Plains - Western Edge
description: The Stonewind Plains begin here as the Tidewoods end, the abrupt transition from forest to open savannah creating a landscape edge as dramatic as a shoreline. The warm, steady breeze off the plains is a shock after the forest's humid stillness, and the smell changes from rich organic complexity to dry grass and far-off stone. The Giant's Teeth are visible as a smudged line to the west on clear days, and the sky above is larger here than in the Tidewoods â€” genuinely large, horizon-to-horizon, with the high weather patterns visible from miles away. Trade routes to Kinsweave follow this edge westward.

name: Tidewoods - Mist Edge
description: The heavy mist of the Ashen Hollows begins to be noticeable here, the cold grey vapor flowing south from the ruins along the forest floor. The temperature drops several degrees and the light acquires the flat, directionless quality of heavy overcast even when the sky above the canopy is clear. Veteran travelers orient by sound rather than sight in mist this thick: bird-calls, the drip of condensation from leaves, and the direction of the Kailian's smell, which carries even through the mist. Sand Wraiths have been sighted in this sector; the patrol boundary for Branmill Cove's watchmen ends half a mile back.

name: Stonewind Plains - Road to Kinsweave
description: The trade road that links Tidebloom Reach to Kinsweave runs through the Stonewind Plains' southern margin, its surface maintained by both regions' joint commercial interest. The road is broad enough for two laden wagons, its surface compacted gravel over packed earth. Milestone posts marked with both the Branmill Cove chainlink device and the Kinsweave tower-and-road symbol line the route at quarter-mile intervals. In good weather the plains here are beautiful â€” rolling golden grass under an enormous sky â€” and in bad weather they are correspondingly exposed, with no shelter between the forest edge and the next waystation.

name: Tidewoods - Deep Forest Crossing
description: The deepest section of the Tidewoods between the Ashen Hollows and the lake-shore settlements is rarely visited except by forest patrols and the determined. The sky-oaks here are among the largest in the Tidewoods â€” old growth untouched by the Deceivers' War's southern fires â€” and their canopy is thick enough to reduce midday to a comfortable dimness. The undergrowth is dense with moisture-dependent plants, and small watercourses cross the path in multiple places, their banks covered in the moss and liverwort that need the constant seep to survive. The forest feels genuinely ancient here in a way that the settled margins do not.

name: Stonewind Plains - Waystation
description: A small waystation maintained jointly by Branmill Cove and Kinsweave provides shelter, water, and emergency supplies at the midpoint of the plains crossing. The station is a stone building with a stable-yard and a cistern fed by a rooftop rain-collection system, staffed by a solitary keeper who manages the horses that the station maintains for message-relay and emergency transport. The keeper is characteristically self-sufficient, the isolation breeding a pragmatism that visitors either find refreshing or alarming depending on their disposition. The station log records every traveler who passes and any unusual observations on the plains.

name: Tidewoods - Forest Road North
description: The north-south forest road that connects the Tidebloom settlements runs through this section of the Tidewoods, linking Tiravel to Myruvane via the interior rather than the lake shore. The road is used by those who prefer the forest's shelter to the shore's exposure, and it carries a steady if modest traffic of silk-traders and instrument-merchants. The Tidewoods sound is different here than near the settlements â€” deeper, more layered, with the calls of birds that are rarely seen closer to human habitation. The road is safe during daylight and carries patrol marks from both Tiravel and Myruvane's regular routes.

name: Tidewoods - Velathenor North Trail
description: The trail from Velathenor's north gate runs through this section of the Tidewoods on its way toward the forest interior and Tiravel. The trail is narrower than the main road â€” single file in places where the undergrowth is thick â€” but well-marked with the carved bark signs that Hasura Elf navigation uses. The humidity is constant and the going is slower than on the road; boots sink slightly into the soft forest floor, and the mud after rain can make sections genuinely difficult. The calls of forest birds overhead track the traveler's progress â€” some species in the Tidewoods appear to follow movement through the canopy, their calls alerting each other to passage below.

name: Tidewoods - Tiravel Forest Road South
description: The road south from Tiravel runs through the Tidewoods' interior, the canopy unbroken above and the forest floor clear of major obstacles on the maintained section. The road connects to both the interior forest routes and, via junction, to the coast road and the Ashen Hollows approaches. Tiravel's Starwind Blade patrols cover this section regularly, and patrol marks on the trees â€” small carved symbols that update with each patrol â€” give experienced travelers a current read on the security situation. The morning of the current patrol is carved on the nearest marker in the Hasura date notation: three days ago.

name: Tidewoods - Canopy Watch Patrol Route
description: This section of the Tidewoods north of Tiravel is the Canopy Watch's primary patrol corridor, the approach most directly toward the Tomb of Kings and the Kin-sense anomaly zone that has been building in patrol reports. The forest here is normal in appearance but the Kin-sense attunement is noticeably fluctuating â€” not dead, but uncertain, like a compass near a magnetic anomaly. Experienced Kin-sense practitioners feel it as a kind of interference rather than absence. The patrol marks on the trees here are more frequent than on the southern routes, and the most recent one is from this morning.

name: Tidewoods - Myruvane-Tiravel Path
description: A forest path connecting Myruvane to the Tiravel approaches, used by the inter-city silk and instrument trade as well as the cultural exchanges between the two Elf-majority settlements. The path is narrower than the main road but more direct, and Pasua Elf wardens maintain it along the Myruvane section. The forest here has a quality of being watched â€” not threatening, but aware â€” that visitors unfamiliar with the Tidewoods sometimes find uncomfortable and that Hasura and Pasua residents consider simply normal. The forest is aware. It has always been aware. This is not a problem unless someone makes it one.

name: Tidewoods - Tiravel East Trail
description: The east trail from Tiravel leads toward the Stonewind Plains, the forest thinning gradually as the sky-oak canopy gives way to the mixed-woodland transition zone. Tiravel residents rarely use this trail voluntarily â€” the Hasura preference for canopy over open sky is strong â€” but it serves the trade traffic bringing iron and stone goods in from the Stonewind Plains' western merchants. The trail is marked at regular intervals, and a Starwind Blade checkpoint is maintained at the forest edge where the trail meets the plains margin.

name: Kailian Shore - South of Velathenor
description: The Kailian shore road south of Velathenor runs between the lake's reedy margin and the Sundrift Veil's lighter woodland, the two ecosystems running parallel for several miles. The road is pleasant in good weather â€” the lake to the east, the trees to the west, the sound of birds and water constant â€” and used by both merchants and travelers seeking a more gradual transition than the forest roads offer. The Sundrift Veil's sunlit canopy and sandy undergrowth are visible through occasional gaps in the roadside vegetation, giving the impression of a more enchanted landscape just behind the trees.

name: Kailian Shore - Forest South Road Junction
description: A road junction on the Kailian shore where the lake-shore road meets the forest trails leading north toward Whistlebank's ruins and south toward Mossgrove's drowned margin. The junction post is notable for its negative directions: both forest routes carry warnings, the northern for Sand Wraiths and the southern for submerged sections and the same hazard. The junction is used as a reference point by chain-field coordinators planning barge operations in the central Kailian section, and it appears on every navigational chart of the region's southern quarter. A small rest shelter provides rain cover.

name: Tidewoods - Hillmeet Approach Trail
description: The trail from Tiravel north toward Hillmeet's ruins diverges from the main patrol road here, its surface still showing the marks of regular patrol traffic but narrowing to single-file width. The ash-smell of the Ashen Hollows is detectable from this point on fair-weather days, a ghost-scent that has persisted for decades without diminishing. The forest on both sides of the trail is normal Tidewoods but carries a watchful quality that increases as the ruins approach. Patrol markers here are more closely spaced than elsewhere and include a distinctive double-slash that means 'report anomaly here specifically if seen.'

name: Kailian Shore - Myruvane Approach
description: The lake-shore path approaching Myruvane from the south passes through the reed-beds where the Kailian's inlet reaches into the forest margin. The path is partially elevated on timber piers across the wettest sections, and the boards are well-maintained â€” Myruvane's commercial traffic depends on this path being passable year-round. The sound of the forest and the lake overlap here in a pleasant acoustic blend, and the light through the reed-heads at dawn creates the kind of display that landscape-minded travelers remember. The Tideweave Bridge is visible from this path once the reeds thin, its silk-weave catching the light.

name: Tidewoods - Myruvane-Foxfen Trail Junction
description: The trail north from Myruvane toward Foxfen's ruins diverges here from the main inter-city path. The junction is clearly marked â€” the Foxfen direction bears the same double-slash warning as the Hillmeet approach, and the Pasua wardens from Myruvane's north gate include this junction in their patrol reports. The trail north is less traveled than the main path and the undergrowth more assertive, requiring occasional ducking under low branches. The Foxfen memorial grove is two hours' travel north at moderate pace, and the full ruins perhaps three. The forest is quiet in this direction in a particular way.

name: Tidewoods - Myruvane-Darnavar Road
description: The road between Myruvane and Darnavar runs through the southern Tidewoods, maintained jointly by both cities. This stretch is in the middle of the maintained section, its surface better than the forest trails but not as good as the stone-paved streets of either settlement. The forest here is less dense than near Tiravel, the canopy lower and the undergrowth more open, reflecting the southern Tidewoods' drier micro-climate. Trade traffic between the two cities is regular enough that a worn secondary path has developed alongside the main road where cart-ruts make the surface unpleasant for foot travel.

name: Kailian Shore - Darnavar East Road
description: The trade road east from Darnavar follows the Kailian's southern shore toward the Sundrift Veil and the connections to Kinsweave. This stretch is the most commercially active road in Tidebloom Reach, its surface heavily used and maintained to a standard that reflects its importance to regional trade. Milestone posts here carry both distances and the water-level indicators that warn travelers when the low-shore sections are at risk of flooding during high-water season. The road is wide enough for two loaded wagons and has passing-places at regular intervals.

name: Sundrift Veil - Forest Margin
description: The Sundrift Veil begins here where the Tidewoods' dense canopy gives way to the lighter, sun-dappled woodland that defines the region south of the Kailian. Sandy soil shows through the sparse undergrowth, and the trees â€” shorter and more widely spaced than the sky-oaks â€” allow direct sunlight to reach the forest floor, creating a warm, golden quality of light that is a dramatic change from the Tidewoods' green dimness. The air smells of warm pine-resin and the lake-breeze from the north, and the sound of birdsong is different â€” more open-country species among the forest species, reflecting the transitional ecology.

name: Sundrift Veil - Trade Road
description: The trade road through the Sundrift Veil connects the Kailian shore settlements to the routes heading west toward Kinsweave and south toward the larger trade networks. The road is well-maintained by the traffic of merchants who prefer this route's gentler landscape to the mountain approaches. The Sundrift Veil's enchanted quality â€” sometimes described as dreamlike, its light having a quality that experienced travelers say makes distances seem shorter and the mood lighter â€” has generated a modest tourism of its own, with merchants planning this leg of their journey for the rest it provides after harder road. A waystation is maintained at the road's midpoint.

name: Sundrift Veil - Southern Edge
description: The southern edge of the Sundrift Veil marks the boundary of the Tidebloom Reach region proper. Beyond here the forest transitions to the mixed terrain that connects this region to the wider continent. The landscape is still beautiful at this edge â€” the golden light of the Veil lingers here longer than seems natural, the last trees of the Veil standing at the road's edge with a deliberate grace â€” and travelers who have walked the full length of Tidebloom Reach often pause at this point to look back north before continuing. The road south is the route by which Tidebloom Reach connects to the rest of Oreka.

name: Tidewoods - Western Patrol Circuit
description: The western patrol circuit of the Tidewoods connects the Stonewind Plains margin to the Branmill Cove and Velathenor north roads, providing a patrol loop that can be walked in a single day by a fit patrol team. This section runs through undisturbed Tidewoods that has been under patrol for long enough that the wildlife accepts human presence without alarm â€” a useful baseline indicator that Kin-sense practitioners rely on. When the animals react to the patrol, something other than the patrol is in the area. The forest here has an old, settled quality that the eastern ruins sections entirely lack.

name: Tidewoods - Northern Mist Zone
description: The mist of the Ashen Hollows extends further south in some seasons than others, and this section of forest represents its maximum reach in bad years. In mist years, the cold grey fog reduces visibility to twenty feet and makes Kin-sense unreliable at range, creating the conditions that forced Branmill Cove's patrol boundary to be pulled south. The trees here show moisture stress from the mist â€” their bark darker than normal, lichens growing that prefer cold and damp â€” and the undergrowth is sparser, the orchids and warmth-loving plants absent. Sand Wraith sightings have been logged in this section twice in the past three years.

name: Tidewoods - Intermediate Forest
description: A stretch of the Tidewoods between the Ashen Hollows mist-zone and the Kailian shore serves as the buffer zone between the haunted northeast and the living settlements. Patrols from Branmill Cove and Tiravel overlap here, their shared presence keeping the intermediate forest reasonably safe during daylight. At night, neither patrol stays in this section, their standing orders being to make the last waypoint before dark. The forest is beautiful and undisturbed-looking, which is the point â€” it is supposed to look safe enough to cross quickly, and it usually is, and occasionally it is not.

name: Tidewoods - Northern Shore Trail
description: The trail along the Kailian's northern shore connects the chain-field approaches between Branmill Cove and Darnavar via the northern margin, an alternative to the interior forest routes used when the shore is navigable. In high-water season the trail is partially submerged and impassable; in low-water season it offers the fastest route between the two cities, passing through reed-beds and open shore sections that give excellent views of the chain-fields in operation. The Mithril Chains are visible here entering the water at regular intervals, their anchor-posts marking the trail with more precision than any waypost.

name: Kailian Shore - Chain-Field View
description: A stretch of the Kailian shore where the chain-fields are at their most visible and productive, this section is the operational center of the Mithril Chain system in the middle section between Branmill Cove and Velathenor. At planting season the barges are visible in formation here, moving slowly along the chains, their crews small figures against the dark silt. The Mithril Chain anchor-posts are spaced every fifty yards along this shore, their rune-inscriptions visible at low water â€” the closest thing Tidebloom Reach has to a monument, the practical infrastructure that keeps the region fed. The Lord of Stone's rune-work and the Lady of the Sea's water seem to cooperate most naturally here.

name: Tidewoods - Northfade Margin
description: The Northfade Veil begins here where the Tidewoods' forest tapers toward the Stonewind Plains' northern approach. The trees are shorter and more widely spaced than the dense Tidewoods interior, their canopy allowing the Stonewind Plains' light to filter through. A light shimmering haze characteristic of the Northfade â€” subtle elemental magic from the Lord of Stone and Wind Lord interaction in this transition zone â€” is sometimes visible on warm afternoons, giving the marginal forest a quality that artists have been trying to capture for generations without quite succeeding. The haze is not dangerous, but Kin-sense practitioners find the combination of elemental signatures here interesting.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Raider Camp Perimeter
description: A wider section of the Foxfen ruins where goblin squatters have thrown up a crude stockade of salvaged beams and canvas between two fire-blackened Elven columns. Smoke rises from cook-fires within; a watchman's crow calls from somewhere above. The path curves in toward the camp's heart.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Watchers' Rest
description: A two-tiered platform cobbled together from Elven wood that did not burn, lashed with new rope. Two human cultists in charred red sashes sit cross-legged on the upper tier, watching the approaches while sharing a water-skin. Their sleeves bear the Flamewarg sun-and-bar marks.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Cook-Fire
description: The camp's central firepit, fed by scavenged Elven timbers that still bear the burn-scars of the original Dómnathar sorcery. A great iron pot simmers with a stew the goblins consider excellent and most humans would decline. A goblin shaman squats beside the fire, reading omens in the rising smoke.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Relic Tent
description: A canvas tent kept deliberately shadowed inside. At the rear, on a low table covered with a velvet cloth, rest the camp's most valuable takings: a rack of scorched Elven harps, a dented silver bracelet, a glass sphere shot through with black veins. This last is a fragment of Sylvara's Shadow Orb -- worth far more than the raiders know. A kobold trapsmith stands watch beside it.

name: Ashen Hollows -- The Captain's Tent
description: The largest tent in the camp, floored with layered rugs salvaged from the burnt cities. A desk of good wood holds a bandit-lord's working papers: tally-sheets of stolen goods, a half-written letter to a 'respected buyer in the Marches.' At the desk sits the Captain himself, a handsome man with unusually elongated ears he explains as a Westerlin birth-trait. He is not Westerlin. He is not, quite, human.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Dark Dwarf Lodge
description: A stout tent, its canvas doubled against cold, pitched a little apart from the goblin tents. Two Dark Dwarves lodge here: the Captain's prized heavy muscle. A small portable forge sits cold in one corner; a wooden rack holds their armor and axes.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Slave Pen
description: A crude enclosure of lashed timber where the camp keeps its occasional captives. Currently two: a Pasua scout with a splinted arm, and a Twin Rivers trader whose caravan was taken last month. A Silentborn guards the pen, posing as a human mercenary -- an act she has been maintaining for the Captain's benefit for six months.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Storeroom
description: A canvas-roofed lean-to built against a surviving wall-section of an Elven storehouse. The camp's supplies are stacked neatly: ration-crates, water-barrels, oil-jars, and a locked strongbox containing the Captain's emergency coin. A hobgoblin sergeant sits at a camp-stool, reading the manifest-ledger by lantern light.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Smuggler's Cut
description: A narrow path cut through the brush behind the camp, leading down to a hidden dock on a concealed arm of Foxfen's old canal. The Captain's goods leave the camp here, on flat-bottomed boats bound for the Marches. Currently the dock is empty; the next shipment is two nights away.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Trade Tent
description: A canvas pavilion where the Captain conducts business with visiting buyers. A low table, two chairs, a folded cloth for displaying goods. A locked chest contains sample-pieces. The pavilion is empty now; the last negotiation concluded yesterday evening and the Captain is still tallying the profit.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Cultist's Tent
description: A low tent the Flamewarg cultists use for their evening prayers. A crude altar holds a crossed-out-sun symbol and a sputtering candle. Sleeping rolls are arranged around the altar in a precise circle. The air smells of old incense and newer lamp-oil.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Scout Bunker
description: A half-dug bunker in the ruin's outer margin, concealed by brush and draped with old Elven canvas. The bunker's single goblin scout watches the south approach through a slit-window. The view commands a long stretch of the Foxfen path.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Relic Stash
description: A concealed pit beneath the scout bunker's floor, accessible only by lifting a canvas rug and prying up two loose boards. Inside, wrapped in oilcloth, are the items the Captain has not yet declared to his partners: four silver bracelets, a sealed jar of elven perfume, and the clean half of Aerith's Flute.

name: Ashen Hollows -- Escape Path
description: A narrow trail slipping away south from the relic stash, through the densest part of the old-Elf tree-fall. Hard to follow without knowing the way. This is the Captain's personal exit, memorized rather than mapped. It emerges, eventually, on the road to the Marches.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Hidden Approach
description: North of the Tomb of Kings approach, the forest thickens until the canopy almost closes overhead. An unmarked path -- hardly a path, really -- winds through the undergrowth. A careful watcher will notice that the nearby trees have been subtly pruned to conceal the line-of-sight from the main road.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Enclave Gate
description: A modest gate of woven sapling-wood stands in a clearing where the forest path ends. Two figures in plain homespun sit on a log bench: an older Half-Dómnathar man who smiles at visitors with genuine, if wary, warmth, and a young Silentborn woman who looks up from her mending. Neither is armed. The welcome is real.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Village Square
description: A small square paved with flat river-stones. A communal well at the center; wooden benches around the periphery. Half-Dómnathar villagers go about their business -- carrying water, hanging laundry, calling to children. Their eyes, in the shade, catch the light at a faintly-wrong angle. No one is hostile. No one has been hostile, to a stranger, in three generations.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Elder's Hall
description: A longhouse of salvaged timber where the village's elders gather for council. Low benches, a central hearth now cold, and a carved wooden chair at the hall's head bearing an empty iron circle worked into its back. The hall is empty at the moment. The village elder is not here.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Common Kitchen
description: A long open-sided kitchen where the village's meals are prepared communally. Two older Half-Dómnathar women chop root vegetables at a long table, speaking quietly about weather and grandchildren. The smell of bread and onions is excellent.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Half-Dómnathar Quarters
description: A row of modest cottages where the village's Half-Dómnathar families live. Kitchen-gardens at each front door; chickens pecking in the lane; a child's wooden toy left on a doorstep. The cottages are well-kept and warm-looking. Normal life happens here.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Silentborn Farmers' Huts
description: A smaller cluster of huts, simpler in construction, where the village's Silentborn farmers live. A few are tending small vegetable-plots; others repair fishing-nets on a stretched frame. They are quieter than their Half-Dómnathar neighbors. They have not forgotten what was done to their grandmothers.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Dark Dwarf Forge
description: A modest forge at the village's edge, tended by the enclave's two Dark Dwarves. The forge is cold today; the smiths are at the water-barrel, sharing a meal. They nod at visitors without hostility, but without particular welcome either.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Shrine of the Fallen
description: A small open shrine of polished stone bearing names in three scripts -- the enclave's dead, across four generations. Half-Dómnathar, Silentborn, and the handful of Kin who have chosen over the years to live here and be buried here. A candle burns beneath each name. No one tends the shrine; it tends itself.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Storehouse
description: A log-built storehouse containing the enclave's communal reserves: sacks of grain, barrels of salted fish, bundles of dried herbs. A ledger on a peg records what has been drawn and by whom. The bookkeeping is honest. The quartermaster is not here.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Hidden Understage
description: A concealed trapdoor behind the Elder's chair in the Elder's Hall drops into a stone-lined chamber below the village. The lamps here are brass, carefully shaded. A desk against the wall holds a neat stack of dispatches in Dómnathar script -- receipts of correspondence that the village's elder has been maintaining with the Spur Tower for twenty-two years.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- The Officer's Chamber
description: A private chamber more richly appointed than the hall above would suggest: good rugs, a writing-desk of dark wood, a weapon-stand holding a Dómnathar-forged longsword. At the desk sits the village's Elder -- or rather, the man who has played the role of Elder for twenty-two years. He is Dómnathar. He has been infiltrating this village since the grandfathers of the current villagers were children.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Loyalist Barracks
description: A small barracks beside the Officer's chamber, housing the three Silentborn who know about the understage and have chosen to side with the Officer. Their cots are neat, their weapons are oiled, and the three are currently on duty: two watch the corridor, a third tends a small work-forge in the corner.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- The Void Conduit
description: A stone chamber whose walls are inlaid with silence-runes. In the center, a basin of absolutely still black water -- a scrying-pool connected directly to the Spur Tower's Void Chamber. Through this pool the Officer reports weekly. Beside the pool stands his enforcer-priest, in blackened leather, waiting for today's communion.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Memory Archive
description: A small study lined with shelves of carefully-bound record-books. Each book contains the village's genealogy for a decade -- the true lineages, as the Officer knows them, with names of informants marked in red and with blood-ties to Spur Tower agents traced back to the founding generation. A Silentborn researcher sits at a desk, updating the current year's record.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Escape Burrow
description: A narrow tunnel dug from the understage outward, toward a concealed exit somewhere in the deep Tidewoods. The tunnel is braced with fresh-cut timbers and maintained well. The Officer has used it twice in twenty-two years. He does not expect to need it again, but one prepares.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Children's House
description: A larger cottage above-ground where the village's children sleep during the day-and-night teaching-cycle by which the enclave educates its young. Several half-dozen children of various ages sit at low tables with slate-boards, learning letters. They look up when strangers enter, shy, curious. None of them know what their grandfather does in the understage.

name: Kin Tharonnath -- Deep Forest Path
description: The escape burrow emerges in a thicket far from the village clearing. The Tidewoods stretch away in all directions. A careful walker could vanish into this forest and never be found; the Officer has relied on this fact through three small crises over the years. Today the path is quiet.
